Key responsibilities include developing programs and events that promote mutual respect among students from diverse backgrounds, maintaining relationships with international student groups, and managing campus-wide initiatives such as cultural ambassador programs and host family schemes. The position requires a commitment to whole-person development and creating a vibrant, inclusive campus environment that aligns with the university's mission of preparing global leaders. This is a distinctive opportunity to shape student experience at an institution transforming into a hub for human-centric technology and social innovation.

title: Senior Project Officer
description: This Senior Project Officer position in the School of Data Science requires a Bachelor's degree holder with at least four years of tertiary education experience to support the Dean through event organization, administrative assistance, and project management including budgeting and grant proposal preparation. Key responsibilities include stakeholder liaison, establishment of research partnerships with Mainland institutions (requiring overseas travel), and strategic duties as assigned. The ideal candidate must demonstrate excellence in written and verbal English and Chinese communication, strong proposal writing skills, and the ability to manage complex projects while fostering institutional collaborations in support of the university's transformation into a hub for human-centric technology and social policy innovation.

title: Assistant Programme Manager II / Senior Programme Officer
description: This position manages the Doctor of Artificial Intelligence Studies programme, overseeing all administrative operations, course scheduling, logistics, budgeting, and quality assurance to ensure smooth programme delivery. The role involves coordinating teaching activities, organizing experiential learning and outreach events, and maintaining relationships with students, faculty, and external stakeholders. Key responsibilities include financial compliance, promotional activities, and preparing programme reports for internal and external reviews. The position requires someone capable of balancing operational excellence with strategic programme development in a newly established doctoral programme positioned within the university's broader transformation toward human-centric technology and innovation.

title: Senior Programme Officer
description: This position supports the Division of Artificial Intelligence as a Senior Programme Officer, responsible for comprehensive programme administration including proposal development, admissions processing, financial management, and quality assurance coordination. Key responsibilities span operational management of teaching and learning activities, student services (registration, visa applications), scheduling, event coordination, and budget administration. The role requires a bachelor's degree with at least four years of tertiary education experience, fluency in English and Chinese (including Putonghua), and a proactive, flexible approach to diverse responsibilities. This position is distinctive in supporting a newly established division focused on human-centric technology and interdisciplinary collaboration within a historically arts-focused institution undergoing digital transformation.

title: HPC System Administrator
description: This position involves managing and maintaining High-Performance Computing (HPC) systems and infrastructure, with responsibilities including daily operations oversight, Linux system administration, user account management, proactive system monitoring, and technical support for faculty and students using HPC resources. The ideal candidate holds a bachelor's degree in Computer Science or related field with substantial experience managing large-scale Linux environments, proficiency in Linux/Unix systems and installation across multiple distributions (RHEL, Rocky, Ubuntu, Debian), and familiarity with research software management tools like conda and Docker/Singularity. Knowledge of HPC job schedulers such as Slurm is preferred, along with strong communication skills for user support. The role is distinctive in combining traditional systems administration with specialized HPC expertise to support the institution's emerging focus on data science and interdisciplinary research.

title: Senior Technical Officer / Technical Officer
department: Department of Digital Arts and Creative Industries
description: This position supports the Department of Digital Arts and Creative Industries by managing and maintaining specialized technical equipment including film production, animation, motion capture, sound recording, and immersive media technologies. The successful candidate must demonstrate strong computational and technical literacy, with the ability to troubleshoot independently, learn new equipment quickly, and perform routine maintenance while providing support to faculty and part-time instructors. The role requires effective communication and liaison work between the department, university IT services, and facilities management to ensure seamless operation of technical resources. This is a distinctive opportunity to work at the intersection of creative education and emerging technologies in a rapidly developing creative industries sector.

title: Administrative Officer
description: This Administrative Officer position supports the Department of Economics' Taught Postgraduate Programmes Office, primarily managing admissions and operations for a Master of Science in International and Development Economics collaboration programme. Key responsibilities include processing applications, verifying qualifications, coordinating promotional campaigns, handling student enquiries, and maintaining programme records. Candidates must have at least two years of work experience (preferably in higher education), strong written and oral communication skills in English and Chinese (Putonghua required), and good teamwork abilities. Preference is given to applicants with understanding of development economics, business backgrounds, and course administration experience, with occasional travel and after-hours work required.

title: Counsellor
description: The Counsellor position in the Office of Student Affairs involves providing individual and group psychological counselling to students, including those with special needs, and conducting psychological assessments to develop tailored support plans. Key responsibilities include designing and delivering wellness programmes, coordinating university-wide mental health initiatives, and supporting student integration across diverse backgrounds. The role requires supervising support staff and advising parents and university personnel on student wellbeing. This is a distinctive position emphasizing whole-person development within a transforming institution combining traditional strengths in arts and social sciences with emerging interdisciplinary research initiatives.

title: (Senior) Administrative Officer, Undergraduate Programmes Office
description: This role provides comprehensive administrative support to the undergraduate programmes office within the Faculty of Business, encompassing admissions, programme promotion, student advising, quality assurance, and accreditation coordination. The ideal candidate holds a recognized degree with at least five years of administrative experience, preferably in higher education, with demonstrated competence in admissions, event management, and advanced computer literacy. Fluency in written and spoken English and Cantonese/Putonghua is essential, alongside the ability to work collaboratively and independently with strong analytical capabilities. The position offers competitive remuneration and comprehensive benefits including annual leave, medical and dental coverage, and mandatory provident fund contributions.

title: Administrative Officer
description: The Administrative Officer will provide comprehensive administrative support to the Department of Philosophy, with particular focus on a newly launched 2-year senior-year undergraduate programme and research activities. Key responsibilities include assisting the Head of Department with programme planning and coordination, managing events and seminars, handling financial and budgeting matters, and providing secretarial support to departmental leadership and committees. The role requires liaising with internal and external parties, coordinating professional development opportunities including internships and workshops, and preparing programme-related reports. This position is distinctive for supporting a highly-regarded philosophy department at a research-focused institution that emphasizes interdisciplinary collaboration and human-centric innovation.

title: Assistant Executive Officer (Office Admin & General Service Team)
description: This position involves providing administrative support to a graduate studies office, encompassing daily operations, human resources, budgeting, and student services. Key responsibilities include offering counter services to postgraduate students and stakeholders, coordinating logistics for meetings and events, and managing office operations. Candidates must have at least three years of administrative experience, ideally in higher education, along with strong analytical and problem-solving abilities, bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese (Cantonese and Putonghua), and advanced MS Office skills. The role is distinctive for its focus on supporting a dedicated graduate studies school within a research-oriented institution, requiring coordination across multiple administrative functions to enhance postgraduate education quality.

title: Senior Information Security Analyst
description: This Senior Information Security Analyst position requires managing the institution's security operations across application development, threat detection, and incident response. Key responsibilities include conducting security testing and threat modeling throughout the software development lifecycle, operating advanced security tools (SAST, DAST, vulnerability management, SIEM, SOAR), investigating cyber incidents, and ensuring compliance with data protection standards. The role is distinctive in combining proactive security architecture guidance—including support for a Security Champions Program—with reactive threat intelligence analysis and cybersecurity awareness training across the university community. A recognized Bachelor's degree is required, along with current expertise in information security practices and emerging threats.

title: Information Manager (Virtualisation Services)
description: This position oversees virtualization infrastructure including server virtualization, Hyper Converged Infrastructure, SAN storage, and IoT systems, with responsibilities spanning capacity planning, lifecycle management, backup policy development, and network supporting systems across the institution. The role requires a Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, IT, or related field with at least 8 years of relevant IT experience, preferably including 3 years in a management capacity, along with strong technical expertise in enterprise-grade virtualization technologies. Key distinctive aspects include involvement in emerging technologies such as container orchestration infrastructure and campus-wide IoT deployment, positioning the role at the intersection of core infrastructure and innovation. The position requires shift work and irregular hours, reflecting the critical nature of infrastructure management in a higher education environment.

title: Assistant Accounting Manager I
description: This Assistant Accounting Manager I position in the Finance Office requires supervising an accounting team responsible for monthly payroll cycles, compiling staff cost budgets across departments, and improving workflow efficiency. The ideal candidate holds an accounting degree and professional certification (HKICPA/ACCA), with at least 10 years of accounting experience including 3+ years in management, strong Excel and accounting software skills, and the ability to manage multiple priorities under pressure. Prior experience in higher education or the non-profit sector is preferred. The role offers competitive salary and benefits including annual leave, medical/dental coverage, and retirement contributions, with review continuing until the position is filled.

title: Senior Administrative Manager (Research Postgraduate Team)
description: This position oversees administrative operations for research postgraduate programmes within the School of Graduate Studies, managing functions ranging from admissions and course registration to student records and financial support. Key responsibilities include supervising day-to-day programme operations, providing administrative support across academic development and quality assurance, and assisting in the formulation of academic policies and programme reviews. The ideal candidate will have at least 10 years of post-qualification experience, preferably in tertiary education, combined with strong analytical, interpersonal, and communication skills, plus fluency in English and Chinese including Putonghua. This role is distinctive in its focus on research postgraduate programmes within an institution positioning itself as a comprehensive hub for interdisciplinary research and global academic collaboration.

title: Visiting English Tutor
department: Centre for English and Additional Languages
description: This visiting position involves running the English Language Support Service at the Centre for English and Additional Languages, where tutors provide writing and speaking support through tutorials, workshops, and self-access materials for undergraduate and postgraduate students. Responsibilities include organizing academic and social events designed to develop students' English skills while facilitating cultural exchange between international tutors and Hong Kong students. Candidates should be recent graduates with a Bachelor's or Master's degree from designated partner institutions and demonstrate high English proficiency (IELTS 8.0 or above). The role is distinctive in emphasizing social engagement and cultural interaction alongside academic language instruction, actively encouraging applicants who are willing to build relationships with local students.

title: Associate Director (Application Development)
description: Reporting to the CIO & University Librarian, this role leads the strategic development and delivery of enterprise-level applications supporting the university's academic, administrative, and research operations. The Associate Director will manage a team of over 16 developers, oversee the full software development lifecycle from requirements through deployment, and drive the migration to a new Enterprise Resource Planning system. The position requires a visionary leader with strong technical expertise, exceptional management and leadership capabilities, and commitment to developing secure, scalable, user-centric solutions aligned with institutional priorities. This is a strategic role demanding both technical depth and the ability to collaborate across departments to address institutional technology challenges.

title: Assistant Administrative Officer (TPg Programme Team)
description: This position supports the School of Graduate Studies by providing comprehensive administrative assistance to taught postgraduate programmes, including student admission, data management, teaching coordination, examinations, and quality assurance. The role encompasses counter services, office administration, and student activity support. Candidates must demonstrate strong bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese (including Putonghua) and competency with Microsoft Office and web applications. The appointment is contract-based for up to 11 months, with salary commensurate with qualifications and experience, and includes standard benefits such as annual leave, medical and dental coverage, and mandatory provident fund contributions.

title: Senior Research Officer/Research Officer
department: Department of Sociology and Social Policy
description: This position involves conducting data analysis and research support for a longitudinal study examining discrimination and health-risk behavior within the Department of Sociology and Social Policy. The successful candidate must hold a master's degree in social or behavioral sciences and demonstrate strong proficiency in statistical programming (Stata, Mplus, or R) alongside solid quantitative research methodology skills. Preference will be given to candidates with expertise in health or aging research and experience handling large-scale or panel datasets. The role is offered on a 12-month fixed-term contract with remuneration based on qualifications and experience, including standard benefits such as leave, medical and dental coverage, and retirement contributions.

title: Assistant Research Officer
description: This position is an Assistant Research Officer within the Department of Accountancy, requiring knowledge of textual analysis using Python or similar programming languages. The role is contract-based initially for up to six months with potential renewal, and the salary will be determined by qualifications and experience. The appointment includes standard benefits such as annual leave, medical and dental coverage, and mandatory provident fund contributions. The position supports the university's commitment to interdisciplinary research and innovation, particularly within its broader initiative to establish itself as a hub for human-centric technology and social policy development.

title: Research Assistant Professor
department: Institute of Insurance and Risk Management
description: The position is a Research Assistant Professor at a research-focused institute established in 2021 that aims to become a leading centre for insurance and risk management research across Hong Kong, mainland China, and the broader region. Primary responsibilities include conducting scholarly research and securing external grants, teaching undergraduate and postgraduate courses, and contributing to institutional service activities. Candidates must hold a PhD in Risk and Insurance or a related discipline obtained within the past five years and demonstrate a strong commitment to quality research. The role is distinctive in its emphasis on practice-oriented research that bridges academic inquiry with industry needs, including organizing forums, conferences, and conducting applied surveys to support Hong Kong's development as a premier international insurance hub.

title: Assistant Professor of Teaching
department: Department of Digital Arts and Creative Industries
description: The Department of Digital Arts and Creative Industries seeks an Assistant Professor of Teaching to deliver courses in digital animation, VR/AR, interactive art, or cinematography at undergraduate and postgraduate levels beginning August 2026. Primary responsibilities include teaching across both degree levels, supervising capstone projects, and contributing to departmental development and service activities. The role is distinctive in its focus on teaching excellence within an interdisciplinary creative hub that bridges film, animation, digital media, and curatorial studies while serving Hong Kong's rapidly expanding creative and cultural industries. The position requires expertise in emerging digital technologies and creative practices to support the university's transformation into a center for human-centric technology and innovation.

title: Assistant Library Officer (Cataloguing and Preservation)
description: This position involves cataloguing and preserving library materials according to international standards while maintaining bibliographic records in the Alma Library System, along with compiling cataloguing statistics and performing regular counter duties including evenings and weekends. The ideal candidate should have cataloguing experience in an academic library setting, proficiency in cataloguing standards such as Resource Description & Access and MARC21, and strong English and Chinese language skills. Experience with integrated library systems, particularly Alma/Primo, is advantageous. The role offers competitive compensation and comprehensive benefits including annual leave, medical and dental coverage, and retirement provisions, with consideration given to candidates across experience levels.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow - Philosophy of Contemporary & Future Science
description: This postdoctoral position supports a five-year Senior Research Fellowship on "Philosophy of Contemporary and Future Science" led by Professor Darrell Rowbottom, with appointments lasting one to two years. The ideal candidate specializes in philosophy of science, formal epistemology, AI, or philosophy of biology, chemistry, or physics, and will collaborate with both philosophers and scientists on an interdisciplinary research project. The Department of Philosophy is recognized as one of Asia's leading philosophy departments, with faculty publishing regularly in top-tier journals and maintaining a strong research culture. This role offers the opportunity to contribute to high-impact research while joining a vibrant postdoctoral community within a university actively transforming into a hub for human-centric technology and social policy research.

title: Senior Lecturer / Lecturer
description: The Department of Translation seeks a Senior Lecturer or Lecturer to teach courses spanning computer-aided translation, business translation, interpreting, translation technology, and cultural studies, with an emphasis on both professional practice and academic discipline in bilingual Chinese-English contexts. Applicants must hold a master's degree in Translation Studies or related fields, possess excellent command of both Chinese and English (with Cantonese and Putonghua proficiency preferred), and demonstrate substantial university teaching experience of at least eight years for the Senior Lecturer position. The role is distinctive for its integrated approach to translation as both a practical profession and rigorous academic field, situated within an institution transitioning toward becoming a hub for human-centric technology and interdisciplinary research. Competitive remuneration and comprehensive benefits including medical, dental, and relocation support are offered.

title: Research Assistant Professor
department: Office of the Faculty of Business
description: This position is a Research Assistant Professor within the Faculty of Business, requiring the holder to conduct independent and collaborative research while teaching undergraduate and postgraduate courses. The ideal candidate will hold a PhD in management science, operations research, or a related field earned within the past five years, with demonstrated research excellence and quality publications. The role involves working across the Office of the Faculty of Business and the Hong Kong Institute of Business Studies, with potential administrative responsibilities. This is a distinctive opportunity within an institution emphasizing human-centric technology, social policies, and interdisciplinary collaboration through its advanced research institute.

title: Part-time Lecturer
department: Centre for English and Additional Languages
description: This part-time position seeks an Academic English Language Lecturer to teach undergraduate courses focused on developing English proficiency and critical thinking skills within a center dedicated to language support. Candidates must have university-level teaching experience, preferably in Hong Kong, and hold at least a master's degree in English Language and Literature or a related field, with a PhD preferred. The role emphasizes expertise in foundational academic English instruction and advanced skills such as academic writing, critical argumentation, seminar discussion, and reflective practices. Compensation is competitive and based on qualifications and experience, with appointments made on a contract basis.

title: Sr. Administrative Officer / Administrative Officer
department: LEO Dr David P. Chan Institute of Data Science
description: This position provides comprehensive administrative support to the Director of a data science institute, managing seminars, conferences, workshops, and knowledge transfer activities while developing strategic partnerships with local and international stakeholders to secure funding. The role encompasses operational oversight including budgeting, procurement, reimbursement, and financial management for programme activities, alongside ensuring compliance with internal processes and regulations. Applicants require a Bachelor's degree with at least 5 years of post-qualification experience in the education sector (or less experience for the Officer rank), fluency in written and spoken English and Chinese including Putonghua, and proficiency in PowerPoint, Adobe Creative Suite, and Chinese word processing. The ideal candidate demonstrates strong teamwork, responsibility, proactive initiative, and independent analytical capability to manage multiple responsibilities efficiently and meet tight deadlines.

title: Assistant Human Resources Officer
description: This position involves providing administrative support to the Human Resources Office and assisting with the coordination and administration of various HR-related projects. The ideal candidate will have relevant working experience in sizable organizations, preferably in higher education, along with strong spoken and written English and Chinese (including Putonghua). The role offers a competitive salary commensurate with qualifications and experience, supplemented by benefits including annual leave, medical and dental coverage, mandatory provident fund, and gratuity. The institution emphasizes equal employment opportunities and commitment to workplace diversity and inclusion.

title: Research Assistant Professor
department: Department of Sociology and Social Policy
description: This position seeks a Research Assistant Professor to join the Department of Sociology and Social Policy, requiring a PhD in Sociology, Social Policy, or computational social science obtained within the past five years, along with demonstrated teaching and research experience. The role emphasizes research productivity and publication output, with applicants expected to submit a comprehensive research portfolio, publication record, and teaching evaluations. The department is internationally recognized for research excellence in sociology and social policy, with particular strength in attracting competitive grants and PhD students, and the position aligns with the university's strategic focus on interdisciplinary collaboration and human-centric research. Candidates should demonstrate strong communication skills and a commitment to contributing to the department's research profile while participating in undergraduate and postgraduate teaching.

title: Assistant Administrative Officer
description: This Assistant Administrative Officer position in the Department of Economics provides comprehensive administrative support for undergraduate programs, including admissions, academic advising, assessments, and student services. Key responsibilities include coordinating student and teaching activities, maintaining departmental records, and managing admission interviews, visa applications, and registration processes. The role requires proficiency in Microsoft Office applications and webpage technology. The position is offered on a 12-month contract basis with potential for reappointment based on performance, with compensation and benefits determined by qualifications and experience.

title: Senior Lecturer / Lecturer
department: Department of Sociology and Social Policy
description: The position involves teaching and research in sociology and social policy at a Hong Kong university, with responsibilities including courses in health and social care, fundraising, and program management within an undergraduate social sciences program. Candidates must hold a PhD in Sociology, Social Policy, Social Work, Health Studies, or related fields, demonstrate strong English communication skills, and have relevant teaching experience. The department is research-active with international publication records and success in securing competitive grants, seeking faculty who can contribute to both undergraduate and postgraduate instruction. This role offers the opportunity to join a department recognized for research excellence and to contribute to the university's broader mission of developing human-centric technology and social policies in an increasingly digital environment.

title: Programme Officer, Taught Postgraduate Programmes Office
department: Office of the Faculty of Business
description: This Programme Officer role supports the smooth operation of taught postgraduate programmes within the Faculty of Business, managing course scheduling, student activities, quality assurance processes, and financial administration. Key responsibilities include coordinating programme logistics, handling student and staff inquiries, preparing reports and data submissions, and liaising with internal and external stakeholders. The ideal candidate holds a bachelor's degree with at least two years of relevant experience preferably in higher education, with strong bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese, excellent report-writing and presentation skills, and preferably experience in video editing and social media promotion. The position requires strong organizational abilities, team collaboration, and managerial experience to manage the diverse administrative demands of postgraduate programme delivery.

title: Associate Director/Senior Manager/Manager
description: This role involves developing and executing the university's internationalisation strategy, including building and maintaining international partnerships and overseeing student mobility programmes. The successful candidate will also be responsible for reviewing and enhancing international student recruitment initiatives. Strong interpersonal, communication, and leadership skills are essential, alongside experience in higher education administration. The position offers flexibility in rank and salary based on qualifications, with competitive benefits including leave, medical coverage, and retirement contributions.

title: Director of Research and Knowledge Transfer
department: Office of Research and Knowledge Transfer
description: This Director role leads the Office of Research and Knowledge Transfer, responsible for developing and executing research strategies that align with the institution's vision while advising senior management on research policy and strategic initiatives. Key responsibilities include overseeing research funding applications, negotiating research partnerships, promoting commercialization of research outputs, and enhancing societal impact through technology and policy innovation. The position requires at least 15 years of higher education experience with demonstrated expertise in research strategy, grant management, and knowledge transfer, alongside strong corporate reporting skills and fluency in English, Chinese, and Putonghua. This is a distinctive leadership role positioned to support the institution's transformation into a global hub for human-centric technology and social policy innovation, with particular emphasis on international representation and regional research base development.

title: Administrative Officer /Assistant Administrative Officer (TPg Admission)
description: This role supports the School of Graduate Studies by managing administrative operations for taught postgraduate programmes, with primary responsibilities including student admissions, registration, document verification, and coordination of outreach activities to mainland China. The position also handles financial tasks such as purchases, reimbursements, and budgeting, while the Administrative Officer level additionally supervises junior staff. Candidates require at least two years of experience in tertiary education administration, preferably in postgraduate admissions and programme promotion, alongside fluent English and Chinese (including Putonghua). The role demands strong initiative, the ability to work under pressure, and collaborative teamwork with a high sense of responsibility.

title: Research Assistant Professor
description: The position is a Research Assistant Professor at the Hong Kong Catastrophic Risk Centre, a philosophy-focused research unit seeking someone with expertise in philosophy of AI and/or ethics of risk. The successful candidate must hold a PhD obtained within the past five years and demonstrate a publication record in leading philosophy journals. The role offers the opportunity to conduct high-impact research within a department recognized as one of Asia's strongest in analytic philosophy, while contributing to the centre's mission of addressing catastrophic risks through philosophical inquiry.

title: Assistant Administrative Manager II/Sr. Administrative Officer
description: This role supports a newly established Division of Artificial Intelligence, requiring an administrative leader to manage external affairs, develop branding strategies, and oversee publicity initiatives across multiple channels including the division's website. The successful candidate will need a Bachelor's degree with at least 5 years of experience in tertiary education, combined with strong bilingual English and Chinese language proficiency and excellent organizational and communication skills. Key responsibilities include organizing events, preparing budgets and reports, and disseminating the division's research achievements and news to various audiences. This is a distinctive opportunity to build administrative infrastructure for an emerging research division within a university undergoing significant transformation toward becoming a hub for human-centric technology and social policy innovation.

title: Associate Professor / Assistant Professor
description: The position is a tenure-track role in the Department of English specializing in Modern Literature, requiring the successful candidate to teach undergraduate and postgraduate courses while supervising student projects, conduct high-impact research and secure competitive grants, and contribute to departmental administration and curriculum development. The appointee should demonstrate strong teaching effectiveness and substantial potential for quality research output in the near term. Candidates with experience in digital humanities, modern literature, or world literatures are particularly valued. The institution emphasizes excellence in both teaching and research, with competitive compensation and benefits packages commensurate with qualifications and experience.

title: Manager (Technology Transfer and Business Development)
department: Office of Research and Knowledge Transfer
description: This role involves developing and executing technology transfer and commercialization strategies for the university, including managing the intellectual property portfolio, identifying innovations worth protecting, and promoting inventions to potential licensees and industry partners across Hong Kong, mainland China, and internationally. Key responsibilities include negotiating licensing agreements, organizing technology marketing initiatives and promotional events, maintaining the technology transfer website and database, and producing marketing materials and corporate reports. The position requires a bachelor's degree or higher in science or engineering, alongside proven experience in technology transfer, business development, and event management, with strong written and verbal communication skills in English and Chinese. The role offers flexibility in appointment level based on candidate experience, with competitive salary and benefits including leave, medical and dental coverage, and retirement contributions.

title: Professor of Teaching / Associate Professor of Teaching / Assistant Professor of Teaching
description: This position is a teaching-focused role at multiple ranks (Assistant, Associate, or Professor) within the Marketing and International Business Department, requiring a doctoral degree in Marketing or a related field and a demonstrated commitment to teaching excellence. Responsibilities include delivering core and elective courses, developing new curricula and innovative teaching methods, programme coordination, student mentoring, and facilitating knowledge transfer activities. The role prioritises educational impact and whole-person student development, with candidates encouraged to pursue teaching grants and contribute to curriculum innovation. Appointment rank and competitive compensation will be determined by qualifications and experience, with benefits including leave, medical and dental coverage, and relocation support.

title: Professor
department: Department of Marketing and International Business
description: This Professor position in Marketing and International Business requires a PhD in Marketing or a related discipline, with a strong publication record in leading journals, substantial teaching experience, and demonstrated success in securing competitive research grants. The successful candidate will be responsible for conducting high-impact research, teaching undergraduate and postgraduate students, and contributing to departmental leadership and course development. Previous administrative experience at the departmental level or above is advantageous, and the appointee is expected to actively pursue RGC and international grant funding. The role offers competitive remuneration and benefits with an initial three-year contract that may lead to longer-term appointment with the possibility of substantiation.

title: Senior Research Engineer
description: This Senior Research Engineer position is based in the Office of Service-Learning at a comprehensive university and involves developing humanitarian technologies through the UN University Hub, focusing on applying AI, data analytics, and IoT to address challenges in developing regions and advance sustainable development goals. The role requires conducting research and design work on impact-oriented humanitarian products while collaborating with UN institute coordinators and interdisciplinary teams of designers and engineers to translate innovative concepts into practical solutions. Key requirements include strong expertise in AI and data analytics, demonstrated interest in humanitarian technology applications, and the ability to contribute to teaching responsibilities, including delivering lectures for a Master's programme in global humanitarian innovation. The position is distinctive for its emphasis on human-centric technology development that bridges academic research with real-world impact in the Global South, offering opportunities to work on diverse projects ranging from medical devices to environmental solutions.

title: Associate Professor / Assistant Professor
description: This position seeks an Associate or Assistant Professor in Finance to contribute to a department committed to liberal arts-based financial education and research excellence. Candidates must hold a PhD in Finance or a related discipline with demonstrated research and teaching potential, and are expected to secure external grants, publish in top-tier journals, teach at undergraduate and postgraduate levels, and engage in service activities. Preferred qualifications include a strong publication record, professional credentials such as CFA certification, administrative or teaching experience, and established connections with Hong Kong financial institutions. The role emphasizes excellent communication skills and active pursuit of competitive research funding from both local and international sources.

title: Professor / Associate Professor / Assistant Professor
description: The Department of History seeks a Professor, Associate Professor, or Assistant Professor specializing in Asia in global contexts, with a PhD in History or related field and a strong record of peer-reviewed publications and teaching excellence. The successful candidate must contribute to the department's strategic priorities in global Asia, public history, and/or digital history, with digital humanities experience considered advantageous. Key responsibilities include conducting high-impact research, securing competitive grant funding, and developing innovative courses. The position offers competitive remuneration commensurate with qualifications, along with comprehensive benefits including leave, medical and dental coverage, and retirement contributions.

title: Research Assistant Professor
description: The position is a Research Assistant Professor in the Department of History focused on global history, with preference given to candidates specializing in underrepresented regions such as South Asia, Southeast Asia, Europe, and the Americas, or those with expertise in digital methods for historical research and teaching. Applicants must hold a PhD in history or a related discipline obtained within the past five years, demonstrate a strong publication record and grant-writing experience, and commit to teaching excellence and institutional service. The role requires publishing in international venues, securing competitive research funding, teaching two to three courses annually in their specialty, and fulfilling administrative responsibilities. Candidates with digital research skills and pedagogical experience will be particularly favored.

title: Assistant Professor
description: The Department of History seeks an Assistant Professor specializing in United States history with a transnational or global perspective, requiring a PhD in History or a related field. The successful candidate must demonstrate excellence in research and teaching, with ability to teach U.S. history courses and contribute to departmental priorities including global Asia, public history, and digital history; experience in digital humanities is advantageous. The position is tenure-track with an initial three-year contract potentially leading to longer-term appointment and substantiation, offering competitive remuneration and comprehensive benefits. The appointee will be expected to actively seek external funding through RGC grants and international competitive schemes.

title: Research Assistant Professor
department: Department of Digital Arts and Creative Industries
description: The position is a fixed-term Research Assistant Professor role in the Department of Digital Arts and Creative Industries focused on teaching and advancing the MA in Creative and Media Industries programme. The appointee will teach graduate-level courses, supervise capstone projects, and contribute to departmental development and service. The ideal candidate holds a PhD in film and media studies with a strong publication record, research awards, and success in securing competitive grants.

title: Senior Administrative Officer / Administrative Officer
description: This position supports the Division of Artificial Intelligence by providing comprehensive administrative services, including financial management, procurement, reimbursements, and budgeting. The ideal candidate holds a Bachelor's degree with relevant tertiary education sector experience (minimum four years for the Senior level) and demonstrates fluent written and verbal English and Chinese proficiency, alongside strong analytical and interpersonal skills. The role is distinctive in supporting a newly established division within a university undergoing transformation into a hub for human-centric technology research and innovation. Competitive remuneration is offered alongside standard fringe benefits including leave, healthcare coverage, and pension contributions.

title: Research Assistant Professor
description: This Research Assistant Professor position in Philosophy seeks a scholar to teach specialized coursework, with particular preference for expertise in Set Theory or the ability to cover existing departmental courses to free a colleague for Set Theory instruction. While Set Theory expertise is preferred, candidates with demonstrated teaching competence in set theory or the ability to teach courses such as Critical Thinking, Philosophy of Artificial Intelligence, or Deductive Logic are also encouraged to apply. The role requires a PhD in Philosophy or a related discipline and offers the opportunity to contribute to a department recognized as a leading center for analytic philosophy in Asia, with particular strengths in aesthetics, ethics, Chinese philosophy, metaphysics, philosophy of mind, and related areas. The position includes involvement with the new Bachelor of Arts in Philosophy and AI Programme launching in 2025-26 and the Master in Arts "Artificial Intelligence and the Future" program.

title: Research Officer
department: The Advanced Institute for Global Chinese Studies
description: This Research Officer position supports the director of an institute focused on global Chinese studies by coordinating research projects across humanities disciplines, with emphasis on Chinese literature and culture. Key responsibilities include organizing seminars and international conferences, producing video content on Chinese topics, editing publications, and managing external communications with contributors and media partners. Candidates should demonstrate experience in project design and operational management, and the role offers competitive salary and comprehensive benefits including leave, medical coverage, and retirement contributions. The position is based at a Hong Kong institution committed to interdisciplinary research and global scholarly collaboration.

title: Chair Professor
description: This Chair Professor position in Accountancy seeks a senior academic leader to spearhead research and teaching initiatives while contributing to departmental and faculty management at a Hong Kong institution. The ideal candidate must hold a PhD in a relevant field with substantial teaching experience, demonstrate strong international research credentials through high-impact publications and competitive grants, and possess proven research leadership capabilities and supervision experience. The role prioritises alignment with the university's strategic focus on AI, data science, and STEM-related accounting research, with preference given to candidates with prior administrative experience at departmental or faculty level. The appointee is expected to maintain excellence in both teaching and research while actively supporting faculty development and institutional service.

title: Senior Programme Officer / Programme Officer
description: This position supports the Doctor of Artificial Intelligence Studies program, requiring management of course scheduling, student logistics, budgeting, and stakeholder relationships across academic and administrative functions. The role demands a bachelor's degree and at least five years of experience in program management, administrative support, or event coordination in tertiary or executive education settings. Key responsibilities include organizing outreach events, coordinating teaching activities, handling financial tasks, and serving as the primary student contact. The ideal candidate is self-motivated, proactive, with strong project management capabilities and experience managing high-level programs or events.

title: Senior Programme Officer / Programme Officer
description: This position involves managing administrative and promotional activities for academic programs within a data science division, including developing marketing materials, updating digital platforms, organizing events, and creating content for social media campaigns to increase engagement and visibility. Candidates should hold a bachelor's degree in marketing, public relations, event management, business administration, or a related field, with at least four years of relevant experience for the senior level role. Essential skills include proficiency with Microsoft Office and Adobe Creative Suite, alongside strong organizational abilities and multitasking capabilities. Preference is given to applicants with knowledge of the higher education sector in Hong Kong and mainland China.

title: Associate Professor of Teaching / Assistant Professor of Teaching / Senior Lecturer
description: The position is for a teaching-focused Associate/Assistant Professor or Senior Lecturer within the School of Graduate Studies, specializing in internationalisation and quality management in higher education. The role encompasses teaching and learning activities, academic program coordination and management, organization of research events and knowledge transfer activities, and related administrative duties. Candidates must hold a PhD in a related discipline and demonstrate at least five years of relevant teaching experience with a track record in academic program development. This appointment supports the university's strategic expansion of postgraduate education and its emphasis on interdisciplinary research, particularly in policy studies, health studies, and higher education.

title: Associate Professor/Assistant Professor
description: The position is an Associate/Assistant Professor role in Artificial Intelligence focused on conducting high-quality research in areas such as natural language processing, large language models, and computer vision, while securing external research funding. Responsibilities include teaching undergraduate and postgraduate courses in data science and AI, managing admissions and programme operations, and performing administrative duties. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree in a relevant field with a strong publication and citation record, good command of both English and Chinese, and ideally possess experience in grant applications and academic programme administration; Associate Professor applicants should have at least six years of tenure-track academic experience.

title: Senior Administrative Officer / Administrative Officer
department: Institute of Insurance and Risk Management
description: This role provides administrative support to the Institute of Insurance and Risk Management and the Department of Operations and Risk Management, handling various departmental activities and operations. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree with at least three years of administrative experience, ideally in higher education, alongside strong bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese (Mandarin) and demonstrated computer literacy, including website maintenance skills. The position requires the ability to work both collaboratively within teams and independently, with salary and rank determined competitively based on qualifications and experience. Additional benefits include annual leave, medical and dental coverage, mandatory provident fund contributions, and gratuity.

title: Part-time Clerk
description: This part-time clerk position with a further education institute involves administrative support work on a fractional basis (25-30 hours weekly) or hourly basis (18 hours weekly), with potential weekend and evening shifts at a central Hong Kong location. Candidates must have secondary school completion as a minimum, with preference given to those holding an associate degree or higher diploma, alongside proficiency in English and Chinese (including Putonghua) and strong technical skills in Microsoft Office applications and Chinese word processing. The role is distinctive for its flexibility in scheduling arrangements and its location within a self-financing educational arm providing various tertiary and continuing education programmes. Immediate availability is preferred, and remuneration will reflect qualifications and experience.

title: Senior Programme Officer/Programme Officer,Taught Postgraduate Programmes Office
description: This position involves managing taught postgraduate programmes within the Faculty of Business, with responsibilities spanning admission and registration processes, course scheduling and operations, financial management, quality assurance coordination, and committee support. The role requires a Bachelor's degree with a minimum of two years' relevant experience in tertiary education (four years for the Senior level), alongside strong proficiency in English and Chinese. Key duties include overseeing programme administration, handling student services from admission through completion, coordinating examinations and events, and preparing institutional reports. The position offers opportunities to lead team members and contribute to the university's strategic transformation as a hub for interdisciplinary research and human-centric innovation.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
department: JC STEM Early Career Research Fellowship for Translation and Application (24/25)
description: This postdoctoral fellowship supports early-career STEM researchers for three years of full-time research at the institution, with potential for an additional year of industry-based translational research attachment. The position offers comprehensive financial support including an annual stipend of up to $530,000 plus a 15% completion gratuity, alongside professional development funding of up to $30,600 annually. The fellowship aims to help researchers advance their careers, expand professional networks, and generate social impact through translational research and innovation. Candidates from around the world are eligible, and the position is particularly suited for researchers interested in bridging academic research with practical, real-world applications in industry settings.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
department: LU Presidential Postdoctoral Research Fellowship Scheme
description: This postdoctoral fellowship scheme welcomes early-career researchers globally who have received their PhD within the past three years and demonstrated strong research potential and publication records. Fellows will work collaboratively within a supportive research environment across the institution's diverse faculties and interdisciplinary institutes, with access to resources, expertise, and mentorship to advance their research goals. The position offers a three-year appointment structure, beginning with a one-year contract with potential extension for two additional years based on satisfactory performance. Appointees are required to pursue external funding through specified local and international grant schemes, positioning them for independent research careers.

title: Chair Professor / Professor / Associate Professor / Assistant Professor
description: This position recruits outstanding scholars at multiple ranks (Chair Professor through Assistant Professor) across all academic disciplines, with particular emphasis on interdisciplinary areas connecting STEM with arts, business, and social sciences—especially sustainable energy, carbon neutrality, sustainable environment, and smart infrastructure. Candidates must hold a PhD, with seniority requirements increasing by rank (from emerging scholars for Assistant Professor to established international reputation for Chair Professor). The university offers competitive packages including potential nomination for government-sponsored STEM professorships or the university's Presidential Early Career Scholars Scheme, which provides enhanced remuneration and start-up grants for qualified early-career researchers.

title: Professor and Director
department: Pan Sutong SH-HK Economic Policy Research Institute
description: The position involves leading a research institute focused on economic policy development across mainland China and Hong Kong, with responsibilities including strategic direction of research operations, stakeholder engagement with academia and industry, and building collaborative partnerships. The successful candidate must be an accomplished scholar with substantial expertise in economic development policy relevant to mainland China and Hong Kong, with demonstrated excellence in research, teaching, and knowledge exchange. The role combines a full professorship in Economics with concurrent directorship of the institute, requiring both academic leadership and external engagement capabilities. This distinctive position sits at the intersection of rigorous scholarly work and applied policy influence, serving as a bridge between two major economic regions.

title: Administrative Officer, Taught Postgraduate Programmes Office
description: This administrative role supports the Taught Postgraduate Programmes Office within the Faculty of Business, with primary responsibilities including relationship building with industry partners and external stakeholders to enhance the faculty's reputation, managing social media platforms (WeChat, Xiaohongshu, LinkedIn), and coordinating promotional campaigns and materials. The ideal candidate holds a Bachelor's degree with at least two years of relevant experience preferably in tertiary education, demonstrates fluency in English and Chinese (including Putonghua), and possesses a proactive, independent approach to work with strong analytical abilities. Key qualities include excellent teamwork and communication skills, a strong sense of responsibility, and the willingness to take on diverse tasks and meet tight deadlines. The position offers competitive salary and comprehensive benefits including annual leave, medical and dental coverage, and retirement provisions.

title: Research Assistant I/II (holding a functional title of Full-Stack Web Developer) in the Division of Restorative Dental Sciences of the Faculty of Dentistry
department: Restorative Dental Sciences of the Faculty of Dentistry (08026)
description: This position is a Full-Stack Web Developer role within the Faculty of Dentistry's Restorative Dental Sciences division, requiring candidates to design, develop, and maintain responsive websites and web applications while conducting research on user behaviors and emerging technologies to inform learning management system development. The ideal candidate holds a Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, or related field, with at least one year of full-stack or frontend development experience using technologies such as Node.js, Express.js, JavaScript, Vue.js, and PostgreSQL, alongside proficiency in responsive design and cross-browser compatibility. Distinctive qualifications include experience with LLM integrations and knowledge of learning management systems, which are preferred. The appointment is temporary for up to one year with renewal possibilities, offers competitive compensation commensurate with qualifications, and provides annual leave and medical benefits.

title: Part-time Research Assistant I/II (holding a functional title of UX/UI designer) in the Division of Restorative Dental Sciences of the Faculty of Dentistry
department: Restorative Dental Sciences of the Faculty of Dentistry (08026)
description: This part-time position seeks a UX/UI designer at the research assistant level to develop responsive websites and web applications for the dental sciences faculty, prioritizing user experience and accessibility. Key responsibilities include conducting user research, designing interactive frontend interfaces, collaborating with stakeholders to translate requirements into functional designs, and maintaining existing applications while ensuring security and cross-browser compatibility. The ideal candidate holds a bachelor's degree in computer science or related field with at least two years of frontend development experience, proficiency in HTML5, CSS3, and JavaScript, expertise in modern frameworks like Vue.js or React, and a strong portfolio of web design work. Prior experience with Learning Management Systems (Moodle, Blackboard) or integrating AI-driven features into applications is particularly valued.

title: Senior Executive Assistant/ Executive Assistant (at the rank of Clerk I/II)
department: School of Computing and Data Science (14700)
description: This position supports the rapid growth of a computing and data science school as a Senior Executive Assistant or Executive Assistant, requiring a Bachelor's degree and at least 3 years of work experience, with fluency in English and Chinese (including Putonghua) and strong organizational and communication skills. Primary responsibilities include coordinating admissions outreach activities, developing marketing campaigns both online and offline, creating promotional materials, and engaging with the student community to support the school's growth. The ideal candidate should be a self-motivated team player with experience in admissions and student liaison, capable of managing multiple tasks independently in a fast-paced environment and working flexibly beyond standard hours. The position is offered on a two-year fixed-term basis with competitive salary, benefits, and contract-end gratuity.

title: Assistant Registrar (to be seconded to GBAICTI as its Vice President (Research Strategy Development & Application))
department: Li Ka Shing Faculty of Medicine (20000)
description: This position is an Assistant Registrar seconded to serve as Vice President of Research Strategy Development & Application at a government-backed clinical research institute operated by the Faculty of Medicine. The role involves directing research infrastructure development (biobanks, laboratories, phase 1 centres), managing funding schemes, evaluating novel research methodologies like real-world data applications, and providing consultation to researchers and industry stakeholders. Candidates must hold a bachelor's degree (preferably higher) in medicine, pharmacy, biotechnology or related fields, with at least 10 years of professional and supervisory experience in clinical research, including demonstrated expertise in clinical development strategies, regulatory requirements, and research infrastructure management. This distinctive position offers the opportunity to shape clinical research strategy at a regional level across Hong Kong and the Greater Bay Area while reporting directly to the CEO.

title: Clerk I / II (holding the functional title of Senior Executive Assistant / Executive Assistant)
description: The position involves providing clerical and administrative support to a School, including programme administration, event coordination, enquiry handling, and record-keeping, with flexibility required for work outside standard office hours. Candidates must have 5 passes in Hong Kong secondary examinations (including English, Chinese, and Mathematics) or equivalent, plus at least 4 years of full-time work experience, with strong bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese, computer literacy, and demonstrated interpersonal skills. A Bachelor's degree qualifies applicants for the Executive Assistant functional title, while higher qualifications and greater experience may lead to consideration for the Senior Executive Assistant role. The appointment is temporary (one year) or fixed-term (two years) with potential renewal based on performance, offered on a competitive salary with standard benefits.

title: Senior Lecturer / Lecturer I/II
description: The Department of Accounting seeks a Senior Lecturer or Lecturer I/II to teach undergraduate and master's courses in financial accounting, managerial accounting, auditing, and company law, starting September 2026, on either a part-time or full-time basis. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree in accounting or a related field, demonstrate strong teaching credentials and/or professional experience, be fluent in English, and show willingness to engage in program administration; experience integrating AI tools into teaching is advantageous. The position offers competitive salary based on qualifications and experience, along with comprehensive benefits including annual leave, medical, dental, and housing provisions, with gratuity available upon completion of a full-time 2-year contract. Applications are reviewed continuously until the position is filled and must be submitted online.

title: Research Assistant Professors
description: The position seeks early-career PhD researchers across disciplines including accounting, economics, finance, information systems, operations management, and related fields, ideally within five years of graduation, to conduct independent and collaborative research while contributing to postgraduate and undergraduate teaching. Applicants should demonstrate strong research potential, ideally with competitive grant-winning experience and significant publications, and will be expected to develop their own research program and secure funding within their host department. The appointment is a fixed-term contract lasting two years with potential extension based on performance and funding availability, with competitive salary and comprehensive benefits. Candidates must submit a statement of interest, draft research proposal for grant funding, curriculum vitae, and three reference letters by 31 March 2026.

description: A newly-established Department of Materials is recruiting faculty at all ranks to build an impact-driven, interdisciplinary research hub focused on materials science and engineering with applications in health, energy, and sustainability. Research areas of particular interest include biomaterials, energy and information materials, sustainable engineering materials, advanced modeling and characterization methods, and AI-enabled materials synthesis. Successful appointees will contribute to the Department's vision by conducting transformative research aligned with global and national priorities, mentoring the next generation of leaders through rigorous interdisciplinary education, and translating research into commercial applications that support economic development and societal impact.

description: This Research Assistant Professor position focuses on wetland ecology and biogeochemistry, specifically investigating coastal wetlands as climate solutions. The appointment is contract-based for up to three years, renewable based on performance and funding availability.

description: The Department of Mathematics seeks promising early-career mathematicians for non-substantiable Research Assistant Professor positions across all mathematical fields, with preference for candidates whose research aligns with existing faculty expertise. Applicants must hold a PhD in mathematics or related disciplines and demonstrate strong research potential and teaching capability. The role emphasizes research with light teaching responsibilities (one course annually at undergraduate or postgraduate level), offered on a three-year renewable contract beginning August 2026. Applications should include a resume, academic credentials, and contact details for at least four referees (three research-focused, one teaching-focused), with review commencing December 1, 2025.

Candidates must hold a PhD in mathematics or a related field and demonstrate an established research record with publications alongside a commitment to teaching excellence. The initial appointment is contract-based for up to three years beginning August 2026, with the possibility of substantiation or longer-term employment thereafter. The department is highly internationally ranked and seeks to recruit accomplished scholars to strengthen its reputation in both research and education.

The fixed-term position, renewable based on performance, begins in August 2026 and offers an opportunity to join a diverse, internationally-recognised department ranked 18th globally.

description: This is a faculty position open to multiple ranks (Professor, Associate Professor, or Assistant Professor) in educational leadership and administration. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree in educational administration or related field, possess a strong research record, and demonstrate commitment to both teaching and scholarship, with senior ranks requiring proven leadership in the research field. Responsibilities include teaching at undergraduate and postgraduate levels, supervising graduate students, conducting research, securing external funding, and contributing to department administration, with potential additional leadership roles in faculty research centers. Positions are offered on initial three-year contracts that may convert to longer-term or substantive appointments, subject to funding and performance.

title: Professor(s) / Associate Professor(s) / Assistant Professor(s) (Foundation in Education and Policy Studies in Education)
department: Department of Educational Administration and Policy
description: The department seeks distinguished scholars at multiple professoriate levels to lead teaching and research in foundation in education and policy studies in education, with particular interest in areas such as policy studies, early childhood education, ethics, sociology and philosophy of education, and AI in education. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree in a relevant discipline, demonstrate strong research accomplishments and publications, and show potential for securing competitive research funding alongside a commitment to teaching excellence. Senior applicants (Professor/Associate Professor) should have established international leadership in their field. The successful appointee(s) will teach at undergraduate and postgraduate levels, supervise graduate students, conduct research, pursue external grants, and contribute to departmental administration, typically on an initial three-year contract basis.

title: Professor / Associate Professor / Assistant Professor (Hospitality and Tourism)
department: School of Hotel and Tourism Management
description: The School of Hotel and Tourism Management seeks tenure-track or tenured faculty at all ranks (Assistant, Associate, or Professor) to conduct high-quality research in hospitality and tourism, publish in top-tier journals, and teach undergraduate and postgraduate courses. Applicants must hold or be completing a PhD in hospitality and tourism or a related field, with Assistant Professor positions initially offered on three-year contracts and Associate/Professor roles potentially available on permanent substantive appointments. For Associate and Professor ranks, candidates with industry experience, expertise in programme development, grants or consultancy track records, and demonstrated academic leadership are particularly preferred. The position is distinctive for its location in an AACSB-accredited school offering Asia's first integrated hospitality and real estate undergraduate programme alongside specialized postgraduate degrees.

title: Professors/ Associate Professors/ Assistant Professors
department: Zhizhen School of Interdisciplinary Mathematical Sciences
description: The Zhizhen School of Interdisciplinary Mathematical Sciences seeks Professors, Associate Professors, and Assistant Professors to build a world-class faculty team conducting high-level research and teaching in mathematics and related disciplines. Candidates must hold a PhD in mathematics or a related field, demonstrate a strong research record with publications, and show commitment to teaching excellence. Positions are offered on initial three-year contracts beginning August 2025, with potential for substantiation or longer-term appointment, and applicants should submit a resume, credentials, and references (at least two research and one teaching) by the rolling application deadline starting May 15, 2025.

title: Research Assistant Professor
description: This Research Assistant Professor position focuses on AI applications in health/medicine, embedded AI systems, autonomous driving, and robotics. Candidates must hold a PhD in Computer Science or a related field and demonstrate strong research capabilities in systems and AI, particularly in one of the specified application areas. The role involves conducting and directing research, securing external funding, co-supervising students, teaching at undergraduate and graduate levels, and contributing to departmental service, with close collaboration expected within the host research group on embedded AI systems. The position is offered on a three-year contract basis.

title: Senior Lecturer(s) / Lecturer(s)
description: The School of Accountancy seeks Senior Lecturer(s) or Lecturer(s) to design and teach undergraduate and postgraduate courses in Linear Algebra and Calculus within an accounting and finance context, while serving as academic advisors and contributing to programme planning. Candidates should hold a doctoral degree in Mathematics, Statistics, Business, Finance, Accounting, or related fields, with demonstrated excellence in teaching and strong English communication skills. Those with expertise in customizing business courses in quantitative methods will be prioritized. The position is offered on a two-year renewable contract basis, commencing August 2025.

title: Professor
description: This professorial position seeks an established scholar to advance the department's mission of producing rigorous, socially impactful social work research and education focused on vulnerable populations in areas such as aging, mental health, and family policy. The ideal candidate will demonstrate a strong publication record in social work research, expertise in academic capacity building and mentorship, and proven ability to lead interdisciplinary and international collaborations. The role emphasizes transformational leadership, commitment to evidence-based practice, and cultural understanding of Hong Kong, China, and broader Asian contexts. Competitive compensation is offered, with potential for permanent appointment for exceptional candidates.

Candidates should hold at least a Bachelor's degree, preferably a Master's in Finance, Economics, or a related field, and must demonstrate fluency in English and Chinese, strong presentation and interpersonal skills, attention to detail, and the ability to work under pressure. The position is offered initially on a two-year contract basis, renewable based on performance and mutual agreement. Shortlisted applicants will be invited to interview.

title: Director, School of Hotel & Tourism Management, CUHK Business School
department: School of Hotel and Tourism Management
description: The position involves serving as Director of a specialized school within a leading Asian business school, with responsibility for academic leadership, administrative oversight, and strategic development of the school's undergraduate and postgraduate programmes in hospitality and real estate management. The ideal candidate should be a visionary scholar with a strong publication record and doctoral qualification in hospitality, real estate, or related disciplines, combined with proven university administration experience and demonstrated excellence in both teaching and research. The role requires exceptional networking and communication skills, with established connections in both the hospitality and real estate industries, and reports directly to the Dean of Business Administration as a key member of the business school's leadership team. The appointment is fixed-term for three years, with international experience outside Hong Kong considered a significant advantage.

title: Project Co-ordinator II
description: This role serves as Project Co-ordinator II for an engineering centre, requiring the appointee to support the Centre Director as secretary, act as a primary liaison with internal and external stakeholders, and manage communications through editing publications and website content. Responsibilities include coordinating events, marketing, and promotional activities while providing administrative and logistical support. The ideal candidate holds a Bachelor's degree or higher in Marketing, Communications, Public Relations, or related field, with at least 3 years of relevant post-qualification experience, fluency in written and spoken English and Chinese (including Putonghua), and demonstrated expertise in stakeholder collaboration, multitasking, and attention to detail. The position is offered on a 2-year renewable contract, with prior experience in tertiary institutions considered advantageous.

title: Executive Officer II
department: Department of Otorhinolaryngology, Head and Neck Surgery
description: The appointee will support the Division of Speech Therapy by assisting programme directors with postgraduate and professional diploma management, overseeing divisional administration (budgeting, human resources, projects), organizing academic events, and providing secretarial and supervisory support. The role requires a bachelor's degree, at least 3 years of postgraduate work experience preferably in academic administration, fluency in English and Chinese, strong organizational and communication skills, and proficiency in standard office software. Essential qualifications include excellent problem-solving abilities, multitasking capacity, and demonstrated responsibility; candidates must submit language and academic certificates or their applications will not be considered. The position is offered on an initial 1-year contract, renewable based on performance, with shortlisted candidates required to complete a written assessment.

title: Clerical Assistant
description: This clerical assistant position in the Estates Management Office involves providing administrative and operational support, maintaining records, responding to inquiries, and performing related duties as needed. Candidates must have completed secondary education, strong word-processing and MS Office skills, fluency in English and Chinese, meticulous attention to detail, and excellent communication abilities, with SAP system experience being advantageous. The role includes a computer skills assessment for shortlisted applicants. The position is offered on an initial two-year renewable contract with performance-based continuation.

title: Project Co-ordinator II
description: This Project Co-ordinator II role supports a residential college within a university, managing admissions, scholarships, financial aid, and exchange programmes while coordinating student activities and serving as secretary to college committees. The ideal candidate holds a bachelor's degree or higher with at least three years of post-qualification experience, ideally in tertiary education, and must demonstrate strong bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese, computer literacy, and excellent interpersonal skills. Prior experience with student admissions, scholarships, or college exchange programmes is highly valued. The position requires occasional work outside standard office hours and is initially offered on a two-year renewable contract basis.

title: Project Co-ordinator II
department: Centre for the Studies of Daoist Culture
description: This position supports a specialized academic centre by managing administrative and financial operations, coordinating research programs, conferences, and public lectures, and providing secretarial assistance to senior leadership. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree in Chinese Culture or related fields with at least two years of administrative experience, ideally in higher education, alongside fluency in English and Chinese and strong proficiency with computer applications.
