The role requires independent working capabilities, ability to handle pressure, excellent organizational and interpersonal skills, with prior research project coordination experience being advantageous. The appointment is initially for two years on a renewable contract basis, contingent on performance.

title: Project Assistant II
description: This position supports the Department of Psychiatry as a Project Assistant II, providing administrative, secretarial, and operational assistance including event organization, student activity logistics, website management, and data compilation. Candidates must have secondary education completion with minimum Grade E/Level 2 in at least 5 HKCEE subjects (or HKDSE equivalent) including Chinese and English, along with strong MS Office proficiency, bilingual fluency, and excellent interpersonal skills. The role is based primarily at Tai Po Hospital with occasional work at other locations, and requires fast typing, the ability to work under pressure, and self-motivation. The appointment is initially contract-based for up to 2 years with possibility of renewal, subject to performance evaluation and mutual agreement.

title: Senior Editor
description: This role involves supporting the Communications and Public Relations Office in driving strategic content creation aligned with institutional priorities, including coordinating content generation across the office, writing and editing various materials, and liaising with internal and external stakeholders. The successful candidate must hold at least a Bachelor's degree (Master's preferred) and demonstrate at least 12 years of post-qualification editorial experience in media or publication, alongside fluent written and spoken English and Chinese including Putonghua. Additional requirements include strong multi-stakeholder communication skills and knowledge of higher education and public sectors in Hong Kong and mainland China, with journalism, communication, or social science background preferred. The position is offered initially on a two-year renewable contract with performance-based renewal.

title: Research Associate(s)
department: Department of Systems Engineering and Engineering Management
description: This Research Associate position supports an AI education initiative aimed at developing student competencies in artificial intelligence across Hong Kong schools. Appointees will design and deliver AI training materials and workshops for teachers and students, analyze school needs to support implementation, and collaborate with academics to create high-quality educational resources. Candidates require a Master's degree (preferably in AI, Computer Science, Engineering, Education, or related fields) with at least four years of relevant experience, strong STEAM education knowledge, excellent communication skills, and fluency in Cantonese, English, and Putonghua; teaching experience, programming proficiency, and deep AI knowledge are highly desirable. The role is offered initially on a one-year renewable contract basis.

title: Works Supervisor I/II (Builder Works) (carrying the job title of ‘Maintenance Officer’)
description: The role involves managing building maintenance and renovation projects while supervising internal staff, conducting site measurements, performing building inspections, and handling various technical duties, with flexibility for overtime and weekend work. Candidates must hold a Diploma or Higher Certificate in Building Studies (or equivalent qualification with 5+ years of relevant site experience), plus at least 3 years of supervisory experience in builder works and staff management, alongside knowledge of facilities management software. Applicants lacking the full supervisory requirement but possessing 1+ year of post-qualification experience may be considered for the lower grade position. The appointment is initially contract-based for up to 2 years with renewal possible, subject to performance review.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
description: Research Assistants or Junior Research Assistants in the Department of Medicine and Therapeutics will conduct translational research on age-related neurodegenerative diseases at a hospital-based neuroscience laboratory, performing molecular assays (Western blot, ELISA, qPCR), cell culture, histology, and in vivo experimentation alongside routine laboratory maintenance. Candidates should hold a Master's or Bachelor's degree in Biological Science or related fields with at least one year of relevant post-qualification experience, strong communication and problem-solving skills, and preferably expertise in molecular assays, in vivo models (MCAO, stereotactic injection, behavioral testing), or programming. The position is offered initially on a one-year renewable contract basis.

title: Project Assistant II
department: Minor Programme in Entrepreneurship and Innovation
description: This Project Assistant II role supports an interdisciplinary entrepreneurship and innovation programme, managing social media and website content, coordinating administrative and logistical tasks for courses and events, liaising with internal and external partners, and providing clerical support for meetings. Candidates must have completed secondary education (Bachelor's degree preferred) with specified HKCEE or HKDSE qualifications in English and Chinese, alongside strong proficiency in Microsoft Office, written and spoken English and Chinese, and excellent communication skills. The position is distinctive in its focus on digital engagement and programme promotion within an innovation-focused academic environment, with graphic design experience as an advantageous add-on. The role is offered on an initial 2-year renewable contract, with applications requiring submission of certified language and academic qualification documents.

title: Executive Officer II [carrying the job title of 'Visitor Centre Development Specialist' (Project‑Based)]
description: This project-based role involves leading the complete development and operation of a rebranded visitor centre for the university, encompassing concept design, construction oversight, and long-term operational management. The successful candidate will manage stakeholder collaboration, develop a sustainable business model with diversified revenue streams, and deliver an exceptional visitor experience through thoughtful retail design and service excellence across physical and digital platforms. The position requires a bachelor's degree in business, marketing, interior design, or related field, plus at least 10 years of experience in retail operations, product development, or brand management, with demonstrated success in managing renovation or new retail projects. Key strengths should include expertise in brand strategy, customer experience design, retail environment setup, and the ability to manage complex projects within budget while ensuring timely delivery and stakeholder engagement.

title: Project Co-ordinator II
description: The Project Co-ordinator II role, reporting to the Faculty Secretary, oversees undergraduate admissions across all schemes and channels, including interview coordination, document validation, and offer confirmation, while also engaging prospective students, supporting admissions events, managing social media content, handling admission scholarships, and assisting newly admitted students. Candidates must hold a bachelor's degree with at least two years of administrative experience (preferably in tertiary education), proficiency in Microsoft 365, PDF, Zoom, and Chinese word-processing, fluency in English and Chinese (Cantonese and Putonghua), and strong organizational, communication, and interpersonal skills; knowledge of graphic design tools is advantageous. The position is offered initially on a two-year renewable contract basis, with shortlisted applicants required to sit a written test.

title: Scientific Officer
description: This Scientific Officer position in the Department of Surgery involves conducting research on innovative RIBOTAC and PROTAC therapeutics, managing laboratory operations, and supervising research staff and students while preparing publications and grant proposals. Candidates must hold a PhD in Biomedical or Analytical Chemistry with at least 2 years of postdoctoral research experience, strong knowledge of Molecular Biology and Analytical Chemistry, and a solid publication record, with laboratory management experience being advantageous. The role is initially offered on a 2-year renewable contract basis with performance-dependent continuation. Applications must be submitted online through the institutional career portal.

title: Clerical Assistant
department: Department of Anatomical and Cellular Pathology
description: This clerical assistant position supports the University Pathology Service at a hospital laboratory, handling daily administrative operations including document management, data entry, specimen tracking, and report processing. The role requires secondary education completion and strong bilingual (English/Chinese) skills in word processing, MS Office applications, and verbal communication. Key attributes include meticulous attention to detail, responsibility, self-motivation, teamwork ability, and capacity to work independently, with duties also encompassing specimen and report delivery within and outside the hospital. The appointment is initially for one year and renewable based on performance.

title: Accountant II (carrying the job title of ‘Assistant Finance Manager’)
description: This role is an Accountant II (Assistant Finance Manager) responsible for overseeing financial controls, managing budgets, and ensuring compliance with funding requirements across various project accounts, including donations and external grants. The successful applicant must hold a Bachelor's degree and professional accounting qualification (HKICPA, ACCA, CA or equivalent) with at least four years of post-qualification experience in auditing and accounting, including full bookkeeping competency. Key requirements include strong analytical and organisational abilities, proficiency in MS Office and both English and Chinese, and the capacity to meet tight deadlines; experience with SAP and PeopleSoft is advantageous. The position involves supervising accounting operations, collaborating with auditors and other stakeholders, implementing internal controls and accounting systems, and managing donation collections, with an initial two-year renewable contract.

title: Executive Officer II (Innovation and Impact Development)
description: This position in the Knowledge Transfer Office requires an Executive Officer to manage strategic innovation initiatives through internal and external stakeholder engagement, administer major funding programmes from planning through reporting, and provide high-level administrative support including meeting coordination and complex report writing. The role demands at least two years of post-qualification executive experience with demonstrated competency in stakeholder negotiation, funding management, and report writing, alongside fluency in English and Chinese, strong organisational and analytical skills, and the ability to work independently under pressure. Key requirements include a Bachelor's degree or above, excellent written and spoken communication abilities, outstanding interpersonal and negotiation skills, and advanced computer literacy. The position is offered on a two-year renewable contract basis, with shortlisted candidates required to complete a written test as part of the selection process.

title: Part-time Project Assistant II
department: Centre for Health Education and Health Promotion
description: This part-time Project Assistant position supports health promotion initiatives through conducting workshops in schools and NGOs, managing data entry and analysis, preparing workshop materials, and providing administrative assistance. Candidates must have completed secondary education with Grade E or above in at least five subjects including Chinese and English, alongside strong bilingual proficiency in written and spoken English and Cantonese, fast word-processing skills, and excellent communication abilities. The role particularly values self-motivation, reliability, and the capacity to work both independently and collaboratively, with prior experience in health-related fields being advantageous. The position is offered on a renewable 9-month contract, requiring submission of a CV and education certificates demonstrating language and academic qualifications to be considered.

title: Project Assistant II (carrying the job title of ‘Assistant Programme Officer’)
description: This position supports graduate programme administration, focusing on admissions, registration, student data processing, and general postgraduate office duties. Candidates must have secondary education completion with specified passes in Chinese and English, at least two years of administrative experience (preferably in higher education), strong typing and computer skills, and fluency in English, Chinese, and Putonghua. The role requires meticulous attention to detail, analytical ability, and the capacity to manage tight deadlines, with shortlisted candidates required to complete a written assessment. The appointment is initially contract-based for up to two years with potential renewal based on performance.

title: Part-time Nurse
description: This part-time nursing role involves supporting research operations through administrative coordination, data collection and management, literature review, and manuscript preparation. Candidates must be registered nurses in Hong Kong with a valid Practising Certificate and at least three years of post-registration clinical experience, along with fluent bilingual communication skills (English and Cantonese) and strong computer proficiency. The position requires the ability to work independently, multitask effectively, and demonstrate high responsibility and self-motivation. The appointment is contract-based for up to two months, with applicants required to submit a CV and academic credentials demonstrating their professional qualifications.

title: Project Assistant II (carrying the job title of ‘Assistant Programme Administrator’)
description: The role involves providing administrative and clerical support to postgraduate nursing programs, including assisting students with admissions enquiries, coordinating program events, and handling general administrative duties. Applicants must have completed secondary education with strong qualifications (Grade E or equivalent in at least 5 subjects including Chinese and English), at least 2 years of relevant program administration experience, and proficiency in word processing and standard computer applications. The position requires strong written and spoken English and Chinese language skills. This is a 2-year fixed contract position with gratuity, renewable based on performance.

title: Part-time Project Co-ordinator II (carrying the job title of 'Promotion Officer')
description: This part-time position coordinates a research project on end-of-life care for people with intellectual disabilities, requiring the incumbent to manage clerical and data support, organize promotional activities, create social media content, and assist with video production. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree, demonstrate fluency in English and Chinese, and have at least three years of post-qualification experience, ideally in marketing or event organization. The role demands strong communication, interpersonal, and time management skills, with the ability to work independently and manage expectations across internal and external stakeholders. The appointment is initially for one year and renewable based on performance.

title: Research Assistant / Junior Research Assistant
description: This position supports research at the intersection of generative AI and architectural heritage, focusing on Lingnan water-adaptive architecture through multi-scalar morphological analysis. The Research Assistant will conduct heritage analysis, develop specialized image datasets with performance-based captioning, fine-tune AI models, and coordinate broader research activities including publications and workshops. Candidates must hold a Master's degree in Architecture, Urban Design, or Data Science with proficiency in GIS and Adobe Suite; experience with generative AI tools and machine learning is highly desirable. The role is initially a three-month renewable contract, with exceptional candidates considered for doctoral study opportunities.

title: Laboratory Assistant
description: This Laboratory Assistant position in the nursing school involves preparing clinical skills equipment, maintaining the simulation centre, supporting school events, and performing administrative tasks like record-keeping and data entry. Candidates must have completed secondary education with specific qualifications in Chinese and English plus three additional subjects, basic computer proficiency, strong interpersonal skills, and the ability to work independently; prior experience with nursing equipment is advantageous. The role is distinctive for combining technical laboratory support with administrative responsibilities in a clinical education setting. Initial appointment is for one year, renewable based on performance.

title: Research Assistant
department: Department of Educational Administration and Policy
description: The position is a Research Assistant supporting a project investigating school networks and system leadership across selected schools in the Greater Bay Area. Candidates must hold a bachelor's degree or higher in Education or Social Sciences with at least one year of research experience in educational contexts, along with fluent English and Chinese language skills and proficiency in survey design, interviews, and data analysis. Key responsibilities include conducting literature reviews, collecting and analyzing data, writing reports, and supporting the Project Leader as needed. The role is offered initially on a one-year contract with potential renewal, and applications are reviewed on a rolling basis until March 31, 2026.

title: Full-time/Part-time Research Assistant
description: This position offers a full-time or part-time research role supporting a project on privacy law and data protection reforms in Hong Kong. The Research Assistant will conduct literature reviews, assist with data collection and stakeholder interviews, analyze research findings, and help prepare outputs and workshop events. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree in Law or related field, demonstrate strong legal research and writing skills, and be fluent in English and Cantonese with subject-matter interest. The initial contract runs for at least six months with potential renewal based on performance and funding availability.

title: Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant position supports a digital humanities initiative combining AI technology with Chinese calligraphy research, utilizing a large-scale dataset of millions of handwritten characters from historical documents. The role involves developing two AI models (an AI Agent for document analysis and a Generative AI model for calligraphy), conducting user studies, applying OCR and computer vision techniques, managing dataset quality, and coordinating with multiple stakeholders including researchers, historians, and museum educators. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree in computer science or related technical fields with at least two years of post-qualification experience, strong expertise in generative AI and computer vision, and demonstrated ability to communicate technical concepts across expert and non-technical audiences. The position is distinctive for its blend of cutting-edge AI research with cultural heritage preservation, offering opportunities to contribute to both academic advancement and practical applications in museum exhibitions and educational settings.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
description: This postdoctoral position supports research in pediatric leukemia genomics, focusing on genetic predisposition and oncogenic mechanisms in childhood acute lymphoblastic leukemia, particularly germline GATA3 variants and CRLF2-rearranged B-cell leukemia. The fellow will conduct independent and collaborative research involving large-scale genomic analyses, functional studies (including CRISPR-Cas9 editing, ChIP-seq, and RNA-seq), manuscript preparation, and mentorship of graduate students, with access to advanced genomic and functional genomics platforms. Applicants must hold a PhD in genetics, molecular biology, bioinformatics, or related field obtained within five years, demonstrate strong publication records in cancer or functional genomics, and possess proficiency in R/Python and genomic analysis pipelines, with preferred experience in molecular biology or genome engineering. The appointment is a two-year renewable contract under the

title: Research Associate(s) / Senior Research Assistant(s) (External Fund)
description: The Department of Psychiatry seeks Research Associates or Senior Research Assistants to support a large-scale two-phase cohort and clinical trial study investigating the relationship between sleep, circadian disturbances, and mental health in adolescents. Candidates must hold a Master's degree (or Bachelor's for Senior Research Assistants) in Social Sciences, Public Health, or related fields, with at least four years of relevant post-qualification experience, fluency in English and Cantonese, and strong organizational and communication skills. Primary responsibilities include overseeing project implementation and timelines, recruiting participants and coordinating data collection with local schools, mentoring junior staff, executing study protocols, and contributing to data analysis and report writing. The position is initially offered on a one-year contract basis, renewable by mutual agreement.

title: Research Assistant
description: This position offers a recent graduate the opportunity to work as a Research Assistant on diverse finance research projects spanning environmental, development, and macroeconomic topics, under the mentorship of Prof. Henry Zhang and international collaborators. The role requires a Bachelor's degree with strong quantitative skills, proficiency in coding (particularly R or Python), familiarity with AI tools, and fluency in English, though formal finance education is not necessary. Key responsibilities include writing R code for data visualization, automating PDF information extraction using APIs, and improving Julia code for equilibrium solvers. The position is offered on a one-year renewable contract and is designed to support candidates interested in pursuing doctoral studies in Economics, Finance, or related fields, with candidates expected to demonstrate strong analytical abilities and responsiveness to feedback.

title: Research Assistant / Junior Research Assistant
department: Department of Otorhinolaryngology, Head and Neck Surgery
description: This research assistant position supports AI-driven research projects in otolaryngology, focusing on diagnostics, rhinology, skull base surgery, and olfactory sciences. The successful candidate will hold a bachelor's degree in a relevant field with strong competency in data analysis, programming (Python or R), and AI healthcare applications, alongside excellent analytical and communication skills. Primary responsibilities include data collection and analysis, collaborating with clinical teams to implement AI innovations, contributing to publications, and providing administrative and basic laboratory support. The role offers a one-year renewable contract providing hands-on experience in a multidisciplinary research environment.

title: Junior Research Assistant
description: Junior Research Assistant positions are available in Orthopaedics and Traumatology, based at a hospital in Hong Kong. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree in Biomedical Sciences, Pharmacology, or related fields, with demonstrated research experience or clinical research interest, and fluency in both Cantonese and English. The role involves supporting team-based research projects, with project management or clinical research experience being advantageous. Appointments are made on a one-year contract basis, renewable based on performance.

title: Executive Officer I (Innovation and Impact Development)
description: This role is an Executive Officer in the Knowledge Transfer Office responsible for leading stakeholder engagement and managing major innovation funding programmes (such as RAISe+ and TSSSU), including application development and post-award administration. Key responsibilities include providing strategic secretarial support to senior committees, preparing impact reports and business cases, coordinating flagship events and partnerships, and managing strategic projects. The successful candidate must hold at least a Bachelor's degree and have 8+ years of post-qualification executive experience with a proven track record in securing competitive research or innovation funding, alongside exceptional skills in report writing, strategic analysis, bilingual communication (English and Chinese), and stakeholder negotiation. The position is offered on an initial 2-year renewable contract basis.

title: Research Associate (6-month contract)
department: Office for Greater Bay Area Developments
description: This six-month contract position supports a university office coordinating Greater Bay Area development initiatives, with a focus on organizing a national marine knowledge competition. The Research Associate will manage project execution, coordinate with mainland and Hong Kong stakeholders, assist committees, and provide logistical support for events and daily operations. Candidates must hold a Master's degree with at least four years of relevant experience, demonstrate fluency in English, Cantonese, and Putonghua, and possess strong project coordination skills—preferably with cross-border government collaborations. The role emphasizes interpersonal communication, organizational ability, and proficiency in standard office software.

title: Project Co-ordinator II
department: Department of Computer Science and Engineering
description: The Project Co-ordinator II role supports a newly established research centre focused on Electronic Design Automation, requiring broad administrative expertise across finance, personnel management, committee support, and research project coordination. The position demands a bachelor's degree, at least two years of project management experience preferably in higher education, fluency in English and Chinese (Cantonese and Putonghua), and strong organisational and interpersonal skills. Key responsibilities include managing budgets and expense claims, coordinating major events and workshops, supporting research assistants, and handling general administrative correspondence. The role is distinctive for its combination of operational management duties with event coordination at a cutting-edge research institute, requiring someone who can work independently under tight deadlines while maintaining high standards of integrity and responsibility.

title: Project Co-ordinator II (Manuscript Editing)
department: The Chinese University of Hong Kong Press
description: This position involves overseeing the complete publication lifecycle of scholarly manuscripts from submission through final proofing, while coordinating with internal teams and external partners including authors, designers, and indexers. The role requires managing administrative responsibilities such as database records, project budgets, file organization, and report preparation. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree in Humanities or Social Sciences with at least three years of relevant editorial experience, along with fluent English and Chinese proficiency, strong communication skills, familiarity with the Chicago Manual of Style, and experience editing traditional Chinese materials. The appointment is initially for one year and renewable based on performance.

title: Executive Officer I/II (carrying the job title of ‘Senior Manager / Manager’), Office of Taught Postgraduate Programmes
description: This position manages admissions and student services for taught postgraduate programmes within a leading business school's faculty. The successful candidate will supervise a team handling admissions data, student recruitment events, and stakeholder coordination while reporting to the Administrative Director. Key requirements include a bachelor's degree, at least 8 years of executive experience (preferably in higher education), strong data analytics and presentation skills, and deep knowledge of the Chinese Mainland education sector. The role is distinctive for its requirement to balance operational efficiency with high-quality outputs across a large, internationally diverse student population, and demands flexibility including weekend work and business travel.

title: Assistant Computer Officer
department: Department of Computer Science and Engineering
description: This role involves establishing and maintaining the department's network infrastructure, Linux servers, and IT systems while developing web applications and providing technical support to staff and students. The ideal candidate holds a Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or related field with demonstrated experience in network administration, Linux server management, and web programming, alongside strong problem-solving abilities. Additional expertise in VPN setup, high-performance computing, or Cisco certifications (CCNA/CCNP) would be advantageous. The position is offered initially on a 2-year renewable contract with performance-based continuation.

title: Executive Officer II
description: This Executive Officer II position supports the Graduate Division and Nursing School by managing postgraduate programmes, coordinating academic activities, handling student matters, and supervising staff. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree, have at least five years of executive experience in academic administration at tertiary institutions, and demonstrate fluent bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese (including Cantonese and Putonghua) along with strong organisational and interpersonal skills. The role is distinctive in requiring tertiary institution experience and preference for background in curriculum management, student affairs, and committee services. The position is offered on an initial two-year renewable contract, with shortlisted applicants required to complete a written test.

title: Transport Assistant II(s) (carrying the job title of ‘Assistant Transport Operations Officer’)
description: The role involves serving as an Assistant Transport Operations Officer responsible for monitoring daily on-campus shuttle operations, assisting with bus scheduling and maintenance arrangements, managing school bus services, and providing administrative and clerical support including filing, data entry, and complaint handling; candidates may also be required to perform driving duties and outdoor inspections. Applicants must have completed secondary education with Grade E/Level 2 or above in at least five HKCEE/HKDSE subjects (including Chinese and English), at least two years of post-qualification clerical and administrative experience preferably in tertiary or transportation sectors, and hold valid driving licences for private cars and light goods vehicles. Additional essential requirements include strong typing proficiency in English and Chinese, good English and Chinese language command, knowledge of vehicle maintenance, and proficiency with Microsoft Office applications. The position offers a five-day working week on a two-year renewable contract with potential for extension based on performance.

title: Project Co-ordinator II(s)
department: The Hong Kong Jockey Club Sports Medicine and Health Sciences Centre
description: The position involves coordinating and executing community sports medicine events, providing secretarial and administrative support to sports medicine associations, and assisting with postgraduate programmes and short courses at a hospital-based centre. Candidates require a bachelor's degree and at least three years of experience in event/project management or administrative support, alongside strong problem-solving, interpersonal, and bilingual communication skills in English and Chinese. The role demands self-initiative, the ability to work independently and multitask, and occasional work beyond standard office hours. Appointments are offered initially on a one-year renewable contract.

title: Executive Officer II (carrying the job title of 'Manager, Content and Event'), Alumni Affairs and Development Office
description: This role manages content creation and event execution for a major business school's alumni office, which serves a global network of over 47,000 graduates. The appointee will oversee all aspects of content production (copywriting, design, video), digital communications across multiple platforms, event logistics, and stakeholder relations, while supervising freelancers and student staff and tracking campaign effectiveness. Key requirements include a Bachelor's degree in communication, marketing, or related fields, along with demonstrated expertise in content creation, event management, and digital communications. The position is distinctive for its dual focus on both strategic alumni engagement and hands-on execution, requiring flexibility, bilingual proficiency (English and Chinese), and the ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously across a sophisticated alumni community spanning 30+ countries.

title: Clerical Assistant(s) (carrying the job title of ‘Operations Assistant’)
description: This Operations Assistant position in the Transport Office combines clerical administrative work with customer-facing and operational duties. Responsibilities include standard office tasks (filing, data entry), counter service with passenger enquiry and complaint handling, management of campus school bus services and facilities, and occasional driving duties as needed. Candidates must have completed secondary education, strong word processing and computer skills, a valid driving licence for cars and light goods vehicles, fluent written and spoken English and Chinese, and excellent interpersonal and customer service abilities. The role is offered on a two-year renewable contract, with preference given to applicants with prior experience in customer service or transportation sectors.

title: Executive Officer II (carrying the job title of 'Manager, CRM and Digital Engagement'), Alumni Affairs and Development Office
description: This role manages customer relationship management (CRM) systems and digital engagement strategies for a business school's Alumni Affairs and Development Office, overseeing CRM platforms, alumni data integrity, event support, and communications to strengthen alumni relations and fundraising efforts. Key responsibilities include maintaining and leveraging CRM tools and databases, generating engagement reports, coordinating internal and external stakeholders on system improvements, and providing comprehensive event management support from planning through post-event analysis. The position requires expertise in CRM systems management, data governance, and project coordination, with flexibility for extended hours and business travel. The role is distinctive in its focus on integrating technology and data management to drive alumni community engagement for a major regional business school with over 47,000 alumni worldwide.

title: Assistant Librarian II (carrying the job title of 'Special Collections Librarian')
department: The Chinese University of Hong Kong Library
description: This position seeks a Special Collections Librarian to manage acquisition, preservation, and outreach for extensive collections of Chinese and Hong Kong studies materials, ranging from ancient oracle bones to modern archives. The role requires reporting to the Head of Special Collections and encompasses donor relations, faculty liaison, exhibition development, cataloging, and regular shift duties at library helpdesks. Candidates must hold a postgraduate qualification in librarianship or information science, possess at least three years of relevant professional experience, and demonstrate expertise in archival standards (DACS, EAD, MARC) and systems like ArchivesSpace, along with strong management, IT, and communication skills. The position is offered initially on a two-year renewable contract, with proficiency in both Chinese and English desirable and advanced degrees in related disciplines advantageous.

title: Assistant Librarian II (carrying the job title of ‘Digital Transformation Services Librarian’)
department: The Chinese University of Hong Kong Library
description: This position seeks a digitally skilled librarian to lead the library's digital transformation strategy, focusing on integrating emerging technologies, managing complex digital projects, and enhancing research services. The role requires developing and implementing digital initiatives across the library, including full-lifecycle web and software application development, managing digitisation services, and driving organisational change. Candidates should hold a degree in computer science or related field plus a postgraduate qualification in librarianship, with at least three years of relevant professional experience, technical proficiency in Python and web development, and familiarity with emerging technologies such as text-mining and computer vision. The position involves both strategic leadership and hands-on technical work, alongside shift duties supporting library helpdesks.

title: Project Co-ordinator II(s) (carrying the job title of ‘Executive, Corporate Communications’)
description: This role seeks experienced communications professionals to join the Communications and Public Relations Office as Project Coordinators, responsible for executing the institution's strategic communication objectives through press event planning, media relations management, and high-quality content creation across multiple formats. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree (preferably in Communications or Journalism), have at least 5 years of professional experience in public relations or corporate communications, and demonstrate fluency in written and spoken English and Chinese (Cantonese and Putonghua). The position emphasizes strong organizational, interpersonal, and analytical skills, with shortlisted applicants required to complete written tests in both English and Chinese. Appointments are made on a 2-year renewable contract basis, contingent on performance and mutual agreement.

title: Research Assistant / Junior Research Assistant (The Hong Kong Genome Project)
description: This position supports a government-backed genomic medicine initiative, requiring a Bachelor's degree in genetics, health sciences, biomedical science, or related fields, with at least two years of relevant experience. The role involves conducting basic research, managing project correspondence, assisting genetic counsellors, recruiting and interviewing study participants in clinical settings, and handling administrative duties. Candidates should be detail-oriented team players with strong English and Chinese language skills, though experience in patient care, clinical research, or digital health technologies is advantageous. The appointment is initially contract-based for one year, renewable based on performance.

title: Research Associate (carrying the job title of “Project Manager”)
description: The Department of Obstetrics and Gynaecology seeks a Research Associate/Project Manager to establish and manage high-quality prospective clinical studies. Candidates must hold a Master's degree in Biology, Biochemistry, Life Science or related field, with at least four years of clinical research experience, fluency in English and Chinese (Cantonese and Putonghua), and strong interpersonal skills. Key responsibilities include developing management systems, securing regulatory approvals, coordinating study documentation, training research sites, managing budgets, monitoring protocol compliance, supervising data collection and validation, and reporting progress to oversight committees and funders. The position is offered initially on a one-year fixed contract, renewable based on performance.

title: Assistant Director of Human Resources (at the rank of Senior Assistant Secretary II)
description: This position leads human resources management functions across recruitment, staff engagement, performance management, and talent development. The appointee will develop and implement HR policies while heading team members to work with academic and administrative units on workforce planning and regulatory compliance, with a particular focus on enhancing performance management and designing leadership development and succession planning frameworks. Candidates must hold an honours degree with strong English and Putonghua fluency, possess senior-level HR or public administration experience (ideally in higher education), and demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of employment law, excellent communication skills, and strong analytical ability. The role is offered on a renewable three-year contract, with shortlisted candidates required to complete a written test.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
department: Department of Anatomical and Cellular Pathology
description: This postdoctoral position focuses on lung cancer research, including genomics, tumor microenvironment investigation, and therapeutic development. Candidates must hold a Ph.D. in Biological or Chemical Sciences with molecular biology experience, or a Ph.D. in Computer Science with bioinformatics expertise, along with demonstrated commitment to research. The fellow will conduct independent research, publish findings, apply for grants, and mentor junior researchers at a hospital research facility. The initial one-year contract is renewable based on performance and mutual agreement.

title: Part-time Research Assistant
description: This part-time research assistant position requires a Bachelor's degree in a health-related field (preferably Nursing) plus two to three years of relevant work experience, with demonstrated capability in conducting and coordinating research studies using both qualitative and quantitative methods. Key responsibilities include assisting with data collection, implementing intervention protocols, performing literature reviews, managing data, and writing reports, while maintaining proficiency in English and Chinese and competence with MS Office and SPSS. The role demands independent work ability and offers a one-year renewable contract commencing immediately, with candidates required to submit their CV, qualification certificates, and supporting documents through the online application portal.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate
department: Department of Anatomical and Cellular Pathology
description: This postdoctoral or research associate position supports breast cancer research, requiring either a PhD or a Master's degree with five years of cancer research experience. The successful candidate will conduct experimental work in cell biology, animal studies, and molecular techniques while collaborating with postgraduate students and supporting the writing of manuscripts and grant proposals at a hospital-based research facility. Key competencies include solid technical expertise in molecular biology, strong research motivation, and data management skills, with next-generation sequencing and bioinformatics experience being advantageous. The initial appointment is for one year on a contract basis, renewable by mutual agreement.

title: Research Assistant / Junior Research Assistant
department: Department of Anatomical and Cellular Pathology
description: This position is a Research Assistant or Junior Research Assistant in pathology conducting cancer research across multiple sites. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree or higher in Biomedical Sciences or related fields, with practical experience in animal handling, tissue culture, and molecular analytical techniques. The role involves collaborative research with department staff and offers an initial one-year contract renewable by mutual agreement, making it suitable for recent graduates seeking postgraduate experience. Required application materials include a CV detailing academic qualifications and research experience, academic certificates, and two reference letters.

title: Research Assistant / Junior Research Assistant (carrying the job title of ‘Project Officer/Assistant Project Officer’)
description: This Research Assistant position in Electronic Engineering requires a Bachelor's degree and preferably two years of tertiary experience, with strong bilingual English-Chinese communication skills and proficiency in MS Office and graphic design. The role involves assisting with program administration, project management, and supporting the organization of postgraduate events and activities. Applicants must demonstrate responsibility, self-motivation, and independent working capability; fresh graduates are eligible. The position is offered on a one-year renewable contract, with applications submitted online via cover letter and CV.

title: Assistant Human Resources Officer
description: This General Clerk II position in the Human Resources Office supports personnel administration, staff leave and benefits management, correspondence handling, and general office operations. Applicants must have secondary education completion with specified HKCEE or HKDSE qualifications in Chinese and English, strong word-processing and computer skills (MS Office proficiency), and fluent written and spoken English and Cantonese. While HR sector experience of two years or more is advantageous, candidates with general administrative backgrounds are welcome, and the role offers contract appointment for up to two years with potential progression to Human Resources Officer for degree-qualified candidates demonstrating career advancement potential.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
description: The Department of Psychiatry seeks Research Assistant(s) or Junior Research Assistant(s) to support a large-scale, two-phase cohort and clinical trial investigating the relationship between sleep, circadian disturbances, and mental health in adolescents. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree or higher in Social Sciences, Public Health, Sciences, or related fields, with proficiency in English and Cantonese, strong organizational skills, and demonstrated enthusiasm for research; prior experience in youth mental health and statistical analysis knowledge are advantageous. Responsibilities include conducting clinical and research interviews, performing neurocognitive and sleep assessments, assisting with psychological intervention development, and supporting data entry and analysis, with on-the-job training provided. Positions are offered initially on a one-year contract basis, commencing as soon as possible and renewable subject to mutual agreement.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) – Cross Cultural Analytics (Data Science, NLP, Computational Linguistics, Computational Humanities)
description: Two full-time postdoctoral fellows are sought for an interdisciplinary project combining data science, NLP, and computational methods to analyze the historical influence of Chinese culture in Europe and North America from 1550–1900 using large-scale text data and AI-driven approaches. Applicants must hold a PhD in Computer Science, NLP, Computational Linguistics, Data Science, or a related technical field, with strong Python programming skills, experience with modern NLP tools and ML frameworks, and demonstrated ability to work with textual data (tokenization, embeddings, transformers, OCR, corpus processing). Responsibilities include developing and applying NLP methods (OCR correction, semantic search, knowledge-graph modeling, graph neural networks), processing multilingual historical corpora, co-authoring research papers for digital humanities venues, and collaborating with an international research team. Preference will be given to candidates with multilingual NLP experience, background in digital text

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Assistant(s)
description: This position invites Postdoctoral Fellows and Research Assistants to join a multidisciplinary team developing AI-enabled orthopaedic technologies, including augmented reality surgical navigation systems, AI-assisted diagnostic platforms, and surgical planning tools leveraging large language models. Postdoctoral candidates should hold a PhD in Biomedical Engineering, Computer Science, or related fields with demonstrated expertise in research proposal writing, publications, and project management, while Research Assistants require a Bachelor's or Master's degree with strong interest in medical technologies and AI. Core responsibilities include preparing grant proposals and manuscripts, coordinating with collaborators, supporting software development and clinical data handling, and managing project reporting and presentations. Appointments are initially made on a one-year contract basis, renewable based on performance, with preference given to candidates with experience in AI/ML, medical imaging, or surgical technology.

title: Technician
description: This technician position supports the Orthopaedic Learning Centre and Department at a hospital, with responsibilities spanning cadaver training coordination, workshop setup, audio-visual and medical equipment maintenance, and website management, requiring flexibility including work beyond standard hours. Candidates must hold a diploma or certificate in a technical field (preferably biological sciences) with at least three years of relevant experience, demonstrating proficiency in computer applications, bilingual competency in Chinese and English, and strong interpersonal and organisational skills. The role emphasises hands-on technical capability, independent and collaborative working, and a detail-oriented, customer-focused approach, with prior experience in audio-visual systems and medical instruments being advantageous. The appointment is initially offered as a one-year renewable contract contingent on performance.

title: Assistant Officer (at the rank of General Clerk II)
description: This General Clerk II position within the Finance Office provides administrative and secretarial support to the Director of Finance, including office coordination, enquiry handling, documentation management, and supervision of Office Assistants. The successful candidate must possess secondary education qualifications with Grade E or equivalent in at least 5 subjects including Chinese and English, along with fast typing skills, fluent bilingual communication abilities (English, Cantonese, and Putonghua), and proficiency in Microsoft Office applications. At least three years of full-time administrative and clerical experience is required, preferably in a tertiary institution setting, with supervisory experience preferred. The position is offered initially on a two-year renewable contract and will involve a written assessment for shortlisted candidates.

title: Assistant Computer Officer
description: The Assistant Computer Officer will provide comprehensive IT support to the Department of Information Engineering, including managing IT infrastructure, designing and operating lab courses, and delivering technical assistance to staff and students during standard and non-office hours. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree in a relevant computing discipline and possess at least 3 years of hands-on experience in system administration, network management, and user support, with demonstrated proficiency in networking, Linux, Windows Server, and security incident handling. The role demands strong troubleshooting abilities, excellent communication skills, self-motivation, and the capacity to manage multiple projects independently or collaboratively while staying current with emerging technologies. Additional expertise in cybersecurity, virtualization, cloud computing, system automation, and GPU management would be advantageous; appointments are offered on a renewable 2-year contract basis.

title: Project Co-ordinator II
description: The Project Co-ordinator II role involves providing comprehensive administrative and secretarial support to a robotics research institute, including managing expenses claims, coordinating human resources matters, maintaining institutional systems, and assisting with research project management under the Director's supervision. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree with at least one year of project management experience, demonstrate fluency in English and Chinese (including Putonghua), and possess strong organizational, problem-solving, and time-management skills to work effectively both independently and collaboratively. The position is distinctive in its requirement for bilingual proficiency and preference for experience in conference organization and accounting. The appointment is offered on an initial two-year contract with renewal possible based on performance.

title: Executive Officer II
department: Hong Kong Institute of Educational Research
description: This role provides executive support to programme co-directors managing self-financed and in-service teacher education programmes, with responsibilities spanning administrative coordination, budgeting, admissions, course scheduling, student services, and programme development. Candidates must hold a bachelor's degree, demonstrate 5+ years of post-qualification executive experience, and possess bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese alongside advanced MS Office skills, strong organisational abilities, and meticulous attention to detail. The position requires flexibility for work outside regular hours and involves compilation of proposals, secretarial services for committees, and quality assurance monitoring. Appointment is made on a renewable 1-year contract, with shortlisted candidates required to complete a written test.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate
description: This postdoctoral or research associate position in journalism and communication seeks a researcher with demonstrated expertise in corporate social responsibility and sustainability communication, ideally grounded in interdisciplinary perspectives spanning communication, business, management, or organizational studies. Candidates must hold a PhD (for postdoctoral fellows) or Master's degree (for research associates) in communication or related fields, with at least four years of relevant post-qualification experience, along with strong English communication skills and proficiency in both quantitative and qualitative research methods. The role involves actively supporting research activities including developing and implementing research protocols, managing data collection and analysis, and disseminating findings through publications and presentations. The position is offered initially on a one-year contract basis, renewable based on performance and funding availability.

title: Research Assistant(s)
description: The Department of Surgery seeks a Research Assistant to support research on machine learning applications for diagnosing and treating cardio-thoracic conditions, particularly thoracic aortic aneurysms and dissections. Candidates should hold a Bachelor's degree or above in Healthcare, Biomedical Engineering, Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, or related fields, with knowledge of cardiovascular anatomy, medical imaging modalities, and demonstrated proficiency in machine learning development. Primary responsibilities include designing and optimizing machine learning systems to enhance diagnostic workflows and standardize reporting for these conditions, alongside research administrative duties. The position is offered on a one-year contract basis, renewable based on performance, and requires strong communication skills and fluency in English.

title: Research Assistant / Junior Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant position in the Department of Finance seeks candidates with a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Actuarial Science, Mathematics, Statistics, Economics, or related fields, along with strong programming proficiency in R, MATLAB, and/or Python. The role involves assisting with actuarial science research and performing assigned duties, with preference given to applicants with prior research experience in actuarial science or statistics. The position is initially offered on a six-month renewable contract, contingent on performance and mutual agreement, and requires applicants to demonstrate their programming capabilities and research background in their application materials.

title: Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant position in nursing requires a Bachelor's degree plus at least two years of relevant experience in nursing, public health, or related disciplines, with demonstrated expertise in quantitative and qualitative research, grant writing, and manuscript preparation. Key responsibilities include initiating and conducting research projects, analyzing data, drafting grant proposals and academic publications, and supporting the School Research Committee administratively. Proficiency in both English and Chinese (written and spoken) is essential. The role is offered on a one-year renewable contract basis and requires submission of a CV and academic certificates demonstrating language proficiency and qualifications.

title: Research Assistant / Junior Research Assistant (carrying the job title of ‘Project Officer/Assistant Project Officer’)
department: Department of Mechanical and Automation Engineering
description: This position offers a research support role within a mechanical engineering department, carrying the title of Project Officer or Assistant Project Officer depending on experience level. Applicants require a bachelor's degree and preferably 2-3 years of tertiary institution experience, along with strong bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese, excellent communication and organizational skills, and proficiency with standard office software and Chinese word processing. The role involves assisting with office administration, project management, and coordinating postgraduate programme events and activities. The appointment is offered on a two-year renewable contract basis, with prior postgraduate programme administration experience valued but not essential.

title: Research Assistant / Junior Research Assistant
description: This role involves supporting sleep and mental health research studies through participant recruitment, conducting clinical and research interviews, data entry and analysis, and other assigned tasks. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree or higher in Psychology, Counselling, Medical Sciences, Social Work, or related fields, be proficient Cantonese speakers with strong communication skills, and demonstrate organization, reliability, and enthusiasm for research and clinical translation in mental and sleep health. Prior experience in psychological or psychiatric research is preferred, and the position requires occasional work outside standard hours and weekends. The appointment is initially a three-month contract, renewable by mutual agreement, with on-the-job training provided.

title: Research Assistant
department: Department of Statistics and Data Science
description: This research assistant position is embedded within a leading equine doping control laboratory while employed by the university's statistics and data science department, offering a unique collaborative environment. The role requires a bachelor's degree in molecular biology, biochemistry, or related field, along with strong bilingual communication skills and preferably hands-on experience with PCR, next-generation sequencing, and mass spectrometry. Primary responsibilities include discovering and validating biomarkers for equine doping control through proteomic and transcriptomic analysis, collaborating with multidisciplinary teams, performing instrument calibration and maintenance, and ensuring compliance with international quality assurance standards. Candidates must demonstrate strong organizational skills and responsibility for managing research data while working in a world-class laboratory setting focused on equine sports integrity and horse welfare.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s)
description: Postdoctoral fellows are sought to conduct nursing and health research, developing new projects, managing ongoing studies, and contributing to grant writing and academic publications. Candidates must hold a PhD in nursing, public health, health-related fields, or social sciences, with demonstrated expertise in quantitative or qualitative research, strong publication records, and fluency in both English and Chinese. The position offers an initial one-year contract, renewable based on performance and agreement. Applicants should apply online through the designated university careers portal.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
description: The position involves supporting research projects in molecular diagnostics and circulating DNA at two facilities, with responsibilities including assisting with molecular testing, data analysis, sample processing, and routine laboratory operations using techniques such as PCR and DNA sequencing. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree in Medical Laboratory Science, Biomedical Science, Life Science, or related fields, with preferred laboratory experience and fluency in written and spoken English and Chinese (Cantonese and Mandarin). The role requires self-motivation, responsibility, and strong teamwork abilities. The appointment is initially for one year on a contract basis, renewable based on performance and mutual agreement.

title: Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant position is based at a hospital facility and supports public health research focused on behavioral and mental health promotion across Hong Kong and mainland China. The role involves coordinating research activities, leading group-based health promotion projects, and conducting data analysis, with on-the-job training provided in research methodology, biostatistics, and epidemiology. Candidates should hold a Bachelor's or Master's degree in public health, biostatistics, epidemiology, psychology, or related fields, with statistical skills and fluency in English and Cantonese; prior research coordination or intervention project experience is advantageous. The position is offered on a one-year renewable contract.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Associate(s) / Research Assistant(s)
description: The Sports Medicine Team seeks Postdoctoral Fellows, Research Associates, or Research Assistants to conduct basic research on musculoskeletal tissue repair, with responsibilities including studying therapeutics effects, identifying novel markers of tendinopathy using transcriptomics and lipidomics, and preparing publications and grant proposals. Candidates must hold a PhD in relevant life science disciplines (Bioinformatics, Biomedical Sciences, Bioengineering, or Biology) with strong molecular and cell biology skills, or alternatively an MPhil with four years' relevant experience or a BSc with relevant experience for lower-level positions. The role offers a distinctive opportunity to combine basic research with the possibility of developing the project into doctoral research, contingent on supervisor agreement. Initial appointment is for one year on a contract basis, renewable by mutual agreement.

title: Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant position supports quantitative marketing research projects within the marketing department under Professor Hu's direction. The role requires a bachelor's or master's degree in economics, statistics, business analytics, or related fields, alongside strong programming skills in Excel, R, and/or Python, excellent English proficiency, and prior experience in quantitative research preferred. Key responsibilities include organizing data collection activities, assisting with literature reviews and data management, and providing administrative research support while maintaining meticulous attention to detail and following precise instructions. The position is initially contracted for six months with potential renewal based on performance, and applicants must submit a CV with academic references and a cover letter detailing their research experience.

title: Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant position in the Department of Surgery requires a Master's degree in Biochemistry, Biological Sciences, or a related field, along with demonstrated expertise in mammalian cell culture and molecular techniques such as DNA/RNA extraction, RT-PCR, cytotoxicity assays, and immunofluorescence imaging. The role involves working directly with clinical specimens, including medical sample collection and processing of patient-derived organoids, making it distinctive for its clinical research focus. The position is offered initially on a one-year contract basis, renewable by mutual agreement, commencing as soon as possible.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Associate(s) / Research Assistant(s)
department: Department of Mechanical and Automation Engineering
description: The Department of Mechanical and Automation Engineering seeks postdoctoral fellows, research associates, or research assistants to develop an integrated unmanned aerial vehicle system and digital traffic platform, with focus areas including UAV control and navigation algorithms, traffic prediction modeling, and system integration. Candidates must hold a PhD in Computer Science, Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Traffic Engineering, or related fields (for postdoctoral positions), or a Master's degree with four years of relevant experience in robotics or AI (for research associate positions), or a Bachelor's degree in these disciplines (for research assistant positions). Postdoctoral fellows and research associates will conduct advanced research and development on unmanned systems optimization and traffic platform algorithms, while research assistants will provide support through data collection, labeling, field testing, and model training. The position is offered on a contract basis for up to two years beginning March 2026, renewable based on performance, and requires submission of a detailed

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Associate(s) / Research Assistant(s) (External Fund)
department: The Jockey Club School of Public Health and Primary Care
description: The school is recruiting Postdoctoral Fellows, Research Associates, and Research Assistants to conduct health research using large administrative datasets. Candidates must hold a PhD in public health, epidemiology, biostatistics, or related fields (for postdoctoral roles), or a Master's degree with relevant experience, and demonstrate proficiency in statistical software and quantitative analysis. Responsibilities include developing research projects, managing datasets, writing proposals and publications, and assisting the Principal Investigator. Positions are initially contracted for one year with the possibility of renewal based on performance.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
description: Applicants with a Master's degree (Research Assistant) or Bachelor's/Diploma (Junior Research Assistant) in biological or medical sciences are sought to support microbiology research. Primary responsibilities include managing bacterial strains and stocks, ordering laboratory supplies, conducting laboratory tests, and performing experiments with associated data collection and reporting. The role offers hands-on experience in microbiology, biochemistry, and molecular biology, with work split between a hospital setting and a science park. The initial one-year contract position is renewable based on mutual agreement.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
department: The Jockey Club School of Public Health and Primary Care
description: These positions support public health research through literature review, data analysis, and project management. Candidates must hold a Master's degree (Research Assistant) or Bachelor's degree (Junior Research Assistant) in Statistics, Public Health, Social Science, or related fields, with proficiency in statistical software (SPSS, STATA, SAS, R) and/or qualitative analysis tools (NVIVO), alongside fluent English and Chinese (Cantonese). Core responsibilities include conducting literature searches and screenings, performing statistical and thematic analyses on quantitative and qualitative data, managing datasets, and preparing reports. Experience in evidence-based medicine, guideline development, or traditional Chinese medicine is advantageous; appointments are initially contract-based for up to six months, with renewal dependent on performance.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
description: This postdoctoral fellowship in finance requires a Ph.D. in Actuarial Science or related field, with demonstrated research excellence, strong English proficiency, and the ability to work both independently and collaboratively. The fellow will conduct high-quality actuarial research, develop research proposals, write articles and reports, and present findings under faculty supervision. The one-year contract position begins in January 2026 and requires submission of a cover letter, CV with complete publication list, research proposal, two recent publications, and three referee contacts through the online application portal.

title: Research Assistant(s)
department: Department of Ophthalmology and Visual Sciences
description: The Department of Ophthalmology and Visual Sciences seeks Research Assistants to support clinical and research programmes across diverse ophthalmic specialties including ocular surface diseases, glaucoma, retinal diseases, and ocular cancers, working with a network of over 200 ophthalmologists across Hong Kong and mainland China. Responsibilities include conducting ophthalmic investigations, extracting medical data, and coordinating patient follow-up schedules for research projects, with opportunities to develop specialized investigative skills and contribute to publications. Candidates should hold a Bachelor's degree, demonstrate proficiency in English and Cantonese, possess research interest in ophthalmology, and show patient care commitment; statistical analysis experience and clinical research background are advantageous. The position is initially offered on a one-year renewable contract, with potential support for postgraduate study and career development.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s)
department: School of Hotel and Tourism Management
description: This postdoctoral position supports early-career researchers in real estate economics, finance, urban economics, and related fields, requiring a PhD (or ABD status with strong research experience) and proficiency in statistical programming and econometrics. Appointees will conduct independent and collaborative research, produce publications for top-tier journals, and contribute to the school's intellectual community. The role is initially a one-year contract (commencing soon or August 2026) renewable by mutual agreement, offering opportunities to collaborate with faculty experts in real estate economics, household finance, behavioral finance, and monetary policy. Applicants must submit a cover letter, CV with publications, research statement, and three referee contacts through the online application system, with rolling review beginning immediately.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
department: Department of Geography and Resource Management
description: This postdoctoral position involves conducting original, high-quality research under the supervision of Prof. Jack Harris in areas such as evolutionary economic geography, sustainable transitions, Green FinTech, and entrepreneurial ecosystems. Candidates must hold a PhD in Human Geography or related disciplines, demonstrate a strong publication record, possess excellent English proficiency, and show capability for both independent and collaborative work. The role is offered on an 18-month contract basis, with the start date flexible until March 1st, 2026. Applicants should submit a cover letter, CV, two-page research plan, and three referee contacts through the online application portal.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
description: This postdoctoral position in a pharmacy research team involves leading a Chinese medicine-funded project with responsibilities spanning research logistics, fieldwork oversight, data management, and mentoring of junior staff. Essential qualifications include a PhD in a healthcare or scientific discipline, strong statistical software proficiency, fluency in Cantonese and English, and proven independent research capability, with preference given to candidates with experience in artificial intelligence, project management, or implementation science. The role requires coordinating multi-site studies, developing educational materials, ensuring data quality across collection through analysis, and organizing training sessions. The initial one-year contract is renewable based on performance.

title: Research Associate
description: This Research Associate position supports a pharmaceutical research project focused on data acquisition, processing, and systems development. The successful candidate will hold a doctoral degree in mathematics, engineering, statistics, pharmaceutical sciences, or related fields, with demonstrated programming expertise and preferably background in pharmacology or systems pharmacology. Key responsibilities include operating web crawlers to collect pharmaceutical data, applying deep learning and language models for data classification and extraction, developing project interfaces, and monitoring progress. The role requires independent work capability, strong organizational and communication skills, and a proven track record of research contributions, with an initial one-year contract renewable based on performance.

title: Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant position supports a Chinese Medicine Development Fund project within a pharmacy school, requiring a Bachelor's degree in Pharmacy or related field and strong proficiency in English-language scientific literature, data management, and office productivity tools. The role involves systematically searching, collecting, organizing, and synthesizing pharmaceutical materials and databases; extracting and standardizing key data points; and maintaining accurate records to support the research team's work. The successful candidate must demonstrate meticulous attention to detail, analytical skills, and the ability to work both independently and collaboratively under tight deadlines. The position is offered initially on a one-year renewable contract basis.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate
description: This postdoctoral or research associate position in nursing requires either a PhD in nursing or a health-related field, or a Master's degree with four years of post-qualification research experience. The role focuses on initiating and developing research projects, conducting empirical studies, analyzing data, and supporting the preparation of grant proposals and academic publications. Candidates must demonstrate strong quantitative and/or qualitative research capabilities with an established publication record, along with fluency in written and spoken English and Chinese including Cantonese. The position is initially offered on a one-year contract basis, renewable based on performance.

title: Research Associate / Research Assistant
description: This position is a Research Associate or Research Assistant role within the Department of Finance, supporting both the department and its Finance for Society Research Centre, which conducts interdisciplinary research in areas including quantitative trading, sustainable finance, and fintech. Candidates must hold a PhD in Finance, Economics, or Business (for Associate level) or a Master's degree in these fields (for Assistant level), along with strong data analysis skills, bilingual English-Chinese proficiency including Cantonese, and demonstrated ability to work independently and collaboratively. Key responsibilities include providing research support, organizing departmental and centre activities, managing administrative tasks, and coordinating with internal and external stakeholders. The role is offered on a two-year renewable contract basis.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate
description: This postdoctoral or research associate position supports a major Chinese music research centre with an extensive archive of recordings, scores, and opera materials. The role combines research administration, grant writing, donor engagement, and event coordination, requiring a doctoral degree in music or related fields (or master's degree with four years' experience), bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese, and demonstrated expertise in fundraising, archival work, and public engagement. Key responsibilities include managing funding applications, developing research impact evidence, coordinating conferences and public events, and maintaining databases while collaborating with faculty on research dissemination. The two-year renewable contract offers an opportunity to support a globally significant research platform established from two longstanding collections dating back to the 1970s.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
description: The position supports preclinical PET/MR imaging research at a newly established neuroscience research institute, requiring a Bachelor's degree in Medical Imaging, Radiography, or a related field. Responsibilities include daily facility operations such as equipment maintenance and calibration, coordinating compliance with campus and animal care services, managing facility bookings and procurement, and ensuring ethical standards for animal research. The ideal candidate should have strong organizational and communication skills, bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese, familiarity with MRI technology, and preferably prior experience in laboratory research and animal handling. The role is offered on an initial one-year contract basis, renewable subject to satisfactory performance.

title: Research Assistant
department: Department of Mechanical and Automation Engineering
description: This Research Assistant position focuses on developing learning-based autonomous robotic systems for surgical procedures, combining simulation and physical hardware work with particular emphasis on multi-arm precision automation and embodied intelligence. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Robotics, or related fields, with demonstrated knowledge of imitation and reinforcement learning, hands-on experience with robotics simulators, and proficiency in deep learning frameworks like PyTorch.
