Key responsibilities include building surgical simulation platforms using Isaac-SIM, implementing state-of-the-art machine learning models for surgical tasks, bridging simulation-to-reality transfer, and conducting real-world robotic experiments. The role offers a one-year renewable contract and is particularly suited for candidates with prior experience in ROS, multi-robot control, soft-body simulation, and physical robotic hardware deployment.

title: Full-time/Part-time Research Assistant
description: This research assistant role supports preventive medicine and healthy lifestyle research initiatives, requiring administrative coordination across grant applications, research activities, and departmental liaisons. The ideal candidate holds a Bachelor's degree in a health or social science field, with at least two years of research administration experience and proficiency in both English and Chinese. Key competencies include strong organizational and communication skills, information technology capability, and the ability to work independently with minimal supervision. The position is offered on a one-year renewable contract basis, with responsibilities including grant application support, event coordination, and stakeholder liaison work across university departments and external organizations.

title: Research Assistant(s)
description: Research assistants are sought to support health-risk behaviour research projects, requiring a bachelor's degree or higher in disciplines such as statistics, psychology, social sciences, or public health, along with fluency in English, Chinese, and Cantonese, strong computer literacy, and excellent organisational and communication skills. Primary responsibilities include implementing interventions, recruiting participants and collecting data, conducting literature reviews, managing data entry, and preparing reports and publications. Candidates must demonstrate self-motivation, responsibility, and the ability to work both independently and collaboratively. Positions are offered on an initial one-year contract basis, renewable based on performance.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
department: Department of Ophthalmology and Visual Sciences
description: The Department of Ophthalmology and Visual Sciences seeks research assistants to support clinical and research programmes across academic, community, and translational research focused on various eye diseases including glaucoma, retinal conditions, and ocular surface disorders. Primary responsibilities include conducting ophthalmic investigations, extracting and managing medical data, and coordinating participant follow-up schedules. Candidates should hold a Bachelor's degree, demonstrate strong responsibility and attention to detail, be proficient in English and Cantonese, and possess genuine interest in medical research; prior clinical or laboratory experience and statistical analysis skills are advantageous. The position offers opportunities to participate in landmark research projects, develop specialized ophthalmic skills, and access postgraduate study pathways, with initial appointment on a one-year renewable contract.

title: Research Assistant
department: Institute for Tissue Engineering and Regenerative Medicine
description: This Research Assistant position supports tissue engineering and regenerative medicine research, requiring a Bachelor's degree in Biomedical Sciences or related field (Master's preferred) and practical expertise in primary cell culture, histology, immunohistochemistry, and data analysis. The role involves conducting research under supervisor guidance, assisting laboratory personnel with technical training, and performing assigned laboratory duties, with on-the-job training provided to develop specialized skills. Candidates must be self-motivated, well-organized, independent workers with strong communication abilities in English and Chinese. The position is offered initially on a one-year contract basis, renewable by mutual agreement.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate
description: This postdoctoral position in the School of Biomedical Sciences seeks a PhD-holder with expertise in stem cell engineering, sequencing/gene editing technology, or regenerative biology for nerve injury research. The successful candidate will support the Principal Investigator in project management, establish and maintain research platforms, supervise junior team members, and contribute to manuscript and grant preparation. Applicants must demonstrate strong publication records, bilingual proficiency in Chinese and English, and the ability to work both independently and collaboratively under pressure. The initial one-year contract is renewable based on performance and mutual agreement.

title: Research Assistant(s)
description: This Research Assistant position in Orthopaedics and Traumatology seeks to advance AI applications in medical fields, with primary focus on optimizing large language models using deep learning frameworks such as PyTorch and TensorFlow, alongside programming in Python and R. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Biomedical Science, Computer Science, or related fields, with demonstrated expertise in unsupervised deep learning, statistical programming, and proficiency in both English and Chinese. Key responsibilities include conducting clinical trials, analyzing medical imaging data, and implementing machine learning solutions in a medical research context. The role is initially a one-year fixed-term contract, renewable based on performance, requiring strong technical skills in Python, JavaScript/Vue.js, and scientific computing libraries, alongside effective documentation and communication abilities.

title: Part-time Research Assistant
description: This part-time Research Assistant position in the School of Pharmacy supports Prof. Joyce You's research team, primarily conducting health economics research involving retrospective data collection and analysis of medical costs, literature reviews, and decision-analytic modeling. Candidates must hold at least a Bachelor's degree in Health Economics or related fields (Master's preferred) and demonstrate bilingual proficiency in Chinese and English alongside strong communication skills. The role encompasses database management, Delphi surveys, and various research support tasks assigned by the principal investigator. Applications require submission of a CV and relevant certificates through the online application portal.

title: Research Assistant / Junior Research Assistant
description: This position seeks a Research Assistant or Junior Research Assistant in the School of Biomedical Sciences to conduct research on nerve injury and repair projects while coordinating laboratory operations. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Medical or Biomedical Sciences and demonstrate at least three years of technical experience in animal studies, particularly microsurgical techniques on rodent, rabbit, or pig models. Additional qualifications include proficiency in tissue sectioning, immunohistochemistry, neuro-electrophysiology, and related techniques, along with strong self-motivation and responsibility. The role is initially offered on a one-year renewable contract contingent on performance and mutual agreement.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s)
department: Department of Geography and Resource Management
description: Postdoctoral fellows are sought to conduct research on urban land use change modelling, climate change adaptation, smart cities, and urban informatics primarily focused on China, Hong Kong, and the Greater Bay Area. Candidates must hold a Ph.D. in Human Geography, Urban Studies/Planning, GIS or related fields, with strong publication records and demonstrated expertise in spatial data analysis using tools such as ArcGIS, R, and Python; experience with cellular automata modelling, agent-based modelling, or geospatial AI is advantageous. Primary responsibilities include executing high-quality research, developing research proposals, publishing articles and reports, and presenting findings. The position offers a one-year renewable contract beginning July 2025, with applications reviewed on a rolling basis.

title: Research Associate / Research Assistant
description: A Research Associate or Research Assistant position is available in a biomedical sciences research group focusing on cellular studies and functional characterization of disease-causing genes. Applicants must hold a Master's degree or above in biomedical research or a related field; Research Associates additionally require at least four years of post-qualification experience. Experience in stem cell culture and CRISPR/Cas9 genome editing is particularly valued. The role is offered on an initial one-year contract basis, renewable based on performance, and candidates should submit a curriculum vitae and reference letter through the online application portal.

title: Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant position in the School of Life Sciences involves participating in a butterfly-related research project, with responsibilities including conducting field and molecular biology experiments, drafting reports, collaborating with team members, and providing administrative support. Applicants require a Bachelor's degree or higher in Biology or a related field, bilingual proficiency in Chinese and English, and demonstrated self-motivation and organizational skills, though laboratory experience is advantageous and fresh graduates are encouraged to apply. The role is initially contracted for one year with potential renewal, and on-the-job training will be provided. Applications must be submitted online through the institutional career portal.

title: Full-time/Part-time Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
department: Department of Ophthalmology and Visual Sciences
description: The Department of Ophthalmology and Visual Sciences seeks full-time or part-time research assistants to support clinical and research programmes focused on ophthalmology and visual sciences, with particular emphasis on Chinese medicine treatments for dry eye disease alongside studies on glaucoma, diabetic retinopathy, and other eye conditions. Candidates should hold a Bachelor's degree in Chinese Medicine, Medicine, Public Health, Statistics, or related life sciences, demonstrate proficiency in English and Cantonese, and possess strong organisational, analytical, and interpersonal skills; registration as a Chinese Medicine Practitioner and relevant clinical or laboratory experience are preferred but not required. Primary responsibilities include assisting with clinical studies (literature reviews, data collection and analysis), laboratory work (specimen handling), and providing logistic support, with potential shift work on evenings, weekends, and public holidays. Initial appointments will be made on a one to two-year contract basis, renewable subject to mutual

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Associate(s)
description: The position is a postdoctoral or research associate role within a neuroscience lab focusing on RNA regulation of brain circuit formation, maintenance, and plasticity using advanced techniques such as 2-photon imaging, optogenetics, and electrophysiology. Candidates must hold a PhD in a life science discipline, demonstrate a strong publication record, and possess practical experience with electrophysiology and/or advanced imaging methods, along with excellent communication and teamwork skills. Key responsibilities include conducting experiments, data analysis, manuscript writing, presenting findings at conferences, and mentoring junior team members. The position offers a supportive research environment with strong mentorship in a top-ranked institution located in a vibrant international city.

title: Research Assistant(s)
description: A Research Assistant position supports a neuroscience laboratory investigating RNA regulation of brain circuit formation and plasticity using advanced techniques including two-photon imaging, optogenetics, and electrophysiology. The role combines hands-on research support—such as animal colony management, solution preparation, and equipment maintenance—with administrative responsibilities including reagent ordering, budget management, and documentation of research findings. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree in life sciences with prior research experience, bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese, and strong organizational and teamwork skills, with particular preference for those with background in molecular biology, neuroscience, electrophysiology, or microscopy. The position offers mentorship and career development within a collaborative, well-resourced research environment.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Assistant(s)
description: The Department of Electronic Engineering seeks Postdoctoral Fellows and Research Assistants for an interdisciplinary smart materials research team developing durable, scalable smart window technology. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree in Chemical Engineering, Electronic Engineering, Materials Engineering, or related fields, with a strong research record, independent work capability, and excellent communication skills; experience in smart materials development, light/heat management, or relevant industry work is preferred. Appointees will assist in designing and developing smart windows, optimizing structures and processes, fabricating and testing prototypes, and collaborating with research staff and students on R&D and technology transfer. The position is initially a one-year contract renewable based on performance and mutual agreement.

title: Research Assistant / Junior Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant or Junior Research Assistant position in the School of Life Sciences supports a research center focused on plant-environment interactions for sustainable agriculture and food security. The role involves conducting laboratory experiments and molecular analyses, managing plant materials, collecting and interpreting experimental data, and maintaining detailed research records while collaborating with team members. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree in Chemistry, Molecular Biology, Plant Biology, or related field, with strong organizational skills, attention to detail, independent work capability, and teamwork aptitude; prior laboratory experience is advantageous. Applicants should submit a comprehensive curriculum vitae and academic certificates through the online application portal.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Assistant
department: Institute for Tissue Engineering and Regenerative Medicine
description: The Institute for Tissue Engineering and Regenerative Medicine seeks a Postdoctoral Fellow or Research Assistant to join a laboratory focused on tendon and bone tissue engineering, with research spanning extracellular matrix-based biomaterials, stem cell and exosome therapies, and biomaterial development. Research Assistants require a Bachelor's degree in Biomedical Science, Biomedical Engineering, or related field, while Postdoctoral Fellows must hold a doctoral degree in these or related disciplines; both positions require bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese, strong organizational skills, and the ability to work collaboratively. Primary responsibilities include conducting research experiments, managing laboratory operations, and training new staff members. Initial appointments are contract-based for one year, commencing as soon as possible, with potential renewal based on mutual agreement.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s)
description: The Department of Biomedical Engineering seeks postdoctoral fellows to conduct interdisciplinary research on brain structure and function under Prof. Sadia Shakil's supervision, involving neuroimaging data analysis, processing, and computational modeling. Candidates must hold a PhD in a relevant field (engineering, computer science, mathematics, or related disciplines) with strong foundations in mathematics and programming, particularly MATLAB and Python. Prior experience with neuroimaging modalities (MRI, fMRI, EEG) and expertise in machine learning or computer vision are highly advantageous, as is demonstrated proficiency in English technical writing. The position is offered on a one-year contract basis, renewable based on performance, with applicants required to submit a CV and statement of interest detailing how their skills align with the role's requirements.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Associates(s)
description: The Department of Electronic Engineering seeks one or more postdoctoral fellows or research associates to conduct research in deep learning and remote sensing, with a focus on creating high-definition maps of Hong Kong using LiDAR, surround-view imagery, and GNSS data. Candidates must hold a PhD in Computer Science, Electronic Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, or a related field, with proven experience in project development, manuscript preparation, and data analysis, plus strong English proficiency. Key responsibilities include initiating research projects, collecting and analyzing data, assisting with publications and reports, and performing tasks assigned by the Principal Investigator. The position is initially offered on a one-year contract basis, renewable based on performance and mutual agreement.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
description: These research positions in electronic engineering seek candidates with a Bachelor's degree to assist with developing deep learning models, managing projects, and collecting/analyzing data within the department. Ideal applicants will demonstrate strong responsibility and attention to detail, analytical and interpersonal skills, bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese, and the ability to work both independently and collaboratively. Key responsibilities include training deep learning models, writing research reports, coordinating with collaborating parties, and performing additional assigned duties. The role is initially offered on a one-year renewable contract, with relevant work experience considered advantageous.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Associate(s) / Senior Research Assistant(s) (External Fund) / Research Assistant(s)
department: Department of Sports Science and Physical Education
description: The Department of Sports Science and Physical Education seeks Postdoctoral Fellows, Research Associates, Senior Research Assistants, or Research Assistants (at varying qualification levels from PhD to Bachelor's degree, depending on position level) to support externally-funded research projects. Responsibilities include liaising with schools and community organizations, planning and delivering project activities, conducting impact assessments, managing vendor relationships, analyzing data, and supervising junior staff. Candidates must demonstrate proficiency in English and Chinese, strong communication and organizational skills, and the ability to work both independently and collaboratively, with prior experience working with persons with disabilities or special needs considered advantageous. Positions are offered on one-year renewable contracts subject to satisfactory performance.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Associate(s)/ Research Assistant(s)
description: Positions are available for Postdoctoral Fellows, Research Associates, and Research Assistants within the Department of Information Engineering's advanced wireless networks laboratory, focusing on wireless networks, stochastic control, and AI/ML applications. Postdoctoral and Associate-level candidates must hold a PhD in a relevant discipline with a demonstrated research track record, while Assistant-level candidates require a Bachelor's degree with strong academic performance and related research experience; hands-on experience with 5G/WiFi systems is advantageous. Appointees will conduct research in the specified areas, with senior-level staff also supervising graduate and undergraduate students. Initial contracts are for one year and renewable by mutual agreement.

title: Research Associate / Research Assistant
description: The Department of Psychiatry seeks a Research Associate or Research Assistant to support computational neuroscience projects that translate systems neuroscience insights into clinical research, with a focus on analyzing brain-wide neural datasets to understand circuit-based neuropathological conditions in animal models and human subjects. Candidates must hold a Master's degree (or Bachelor's for the Assistant role) in Computational Neuroscience or related quantitative disciplines, with demonstrated programming skills in data analysis and modeling, proficiency in English, and strong organizational and collaborative abilities; experience with Python, machine learning, and applied statistics is advantageous. The role involves analyzing neural and behavioral data, developing analytical methodologies, disseminating findings, and collaborating across research teams, with opportunities for training, international exposure, and publication. The position is offered on a one-year renewable contract basis.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Associate(s) / Research Assistant(s)
description: The Gerald Choa Neuroscience Institute seeks postdoctoral fellows, research associates, or research assistants to conduct auditory neuroscience research, including studies on auditory perception, processing, pathology, and cochlear implants. Candidates must hold a PhD in neuroscience or related fields, demonstrate proficiency in Python or MATLAB, possess strong data analysis and signal processing skills, and be willing to work with both human participants and animal models. Key responsibilities include conducting experiments, supporting research design and analysis, collaborating with team members, and mentoring junior researchers. The position offers access to state-of-the-art facilities and opportunities for professional development within a collaborative research environment.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
description: The School of Architecture seeks Research Assistant(s) or Junior Research Assistant(s) to support architectural and multidisciplinary research projects in Hong Kong on a one-year renewable contract. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree or higher in Architecture, Landscape Architecture, Design, or related disciplines, with proficiency in English and Chinese, strong technical skills in design software (AutoCAD, Photoshop, InDesign, SketchUp, Microsoft Office), and demonstrated interest in interdisciplinary research bridging humanities and life sciences. Responsibilities include project execution and administration, architectural research and design production, report preparation, field investigations and stakeholder engagement, and miscellaneous research activities as directed by the team. Applicants must submit a statement of interest, CV, and design portfolio through the online application system.

title: Research Assistant
department: Centre for Clinical Research and Biostatistics
description: This Research Assistant position supports the Centre for Clinical Research and Biostatistics in coordinating clinical research and data management projects with both local and international scope, particularly focused on pharmaceutical and medical device development. The role requires a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Science, strong organisational and project management skills, and proficiency in English, with preferred knowledge of statistics and programming. Key responsibilities include administrative project support, assistance with clinical research initiatives, involvement in protocol and case report form development, data management, and multi-centre study coordination. The position is offered on a one-year renewable contract basis.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate
description: This postdoctoral position in the Multimedia Communications Laboratory seeks a researcher with a PhD in a relevant discipline and demonstrated research experience to conduct independent research in mobile networking, Internet protocols, and machine learning. The role combines research responsibilities with co-supervision of graduate and undergraduate students. The position is offered initially on a one-year contract with the possibility of renewal based on mutual agreement.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
description: This postdoctoral position focuses on investigating gene regulation in skeletal muscle stem cells, muscle regeneration, and aging within an orthopaedic research group. Candidates must hold a PhD in Biology, Biomedical Science, Bioinformatics, or related fields obtained within the last five years, with a strong publication record and demonstrated ability to work independently and lead others. Responsibilities include conducting experiments and computational analyses, interpreting research findings, contributing to grant proposals and manuscripts, and mentoring junior group members. The role offers a one-year renewable contract.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Associate(s)
description: This position seeks postdoctoral fellows or research associates to conduct interdisciplinary research on brain structure and function under Dr. Sadia Shakil in a top-ranked biomedical engineering department. Candidates must hold a PhD in a relevant field (or a Master's degree plus four years of work experience) from a recognized institution, with strong foundations in mathematics, statistics, programming (MATLAB/Python), neuroimaging techniques (MRI/fMRI/EEG), and machine learning/AI. The role emphasizes analysis and model development combining multiple disciplines, and applicants should demonstrate strong English communication and technical writing abilities. Positions are available immediately on renewable one-year contracts.

title: Research Assistant(s)
description: The position is for a Research Assistant supporting a large-scale AI education initiative aimed at integrating artificial intelligence into Hong Kong secondary school curricula. Core responsibilities include developing bilingual educational materials, creating hands-on courseware using programming languages and AI frameworks, and delivering training to schools and students. Applicants must hold a Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, AI, Data Science or related fields, with strong English communication skills; desirable qualifications include AI expertise, Python proficiency, teaching experience, and Chinese language abilities. The role is offered initially on a two-year renewable contract basis.

title: Research Assistant
description: A Research Assistant is sought for clinical research in orthopaedics, requiring a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Biomedical Sciences, Biology, or related fields, along with proven research experience and fluency in English and Cantonese. The role involves managing clinical trials, handling tissue samples, obtaining research ethics approvals, coordinating projects, and preparing manuscripts, with prior experience in blood-taking and clinical data analysis being advantageous. The position is based at a hospital and focuses on sports medicine and osteoarthritis research, requiring independent work capability and collaborative team engagement. The appointment is initially a one-year contract, renewable based on performance.

title: Senior Research Assistant(s) (External Fund) / Research Assistant(s)
description: The School of Architecture seeks two research positions to join a team conducting data-driven urban research in Hong Kong, working under Prof. Jeroen van Ameijde. The roles focus on studying public space activity patterns, improving public housing estates, and understanding elderly residents' needs in dense urban environments using advanced computational methods. Candidates must hold a Master's degree in Architecture, Urban Design, Urban Analytics, or Computer Science with four years of relevant experience, plus demonstrated expertise in 3D modelling (Rhino3D, Grasshopper, Python) and strong English communication skills. Senior Research Assistants will lead computer vision methodology development and manage data collection and analysis, while Research Assistants will support with site coordination, digital modelling, and spatial analysis; both positions offer substantial opportunities for skill development, co-authored publications, and preparation for doctoral study or academic careers.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
department: Centre for Cardiovascular Genomics and Medicine
description: This postdoctoral position involves conducting basic and clinical research in cardiovascular diseases with a focus on translational discoveries and precision medicine development within a dedicated genomics centre. The successful candidate will hold a PhD in biomedical science, bioinformatics, statistics, or related fields, with proficiency in both English and Cantonese, and ideally possess prior experience in genetics, genomics, statistics, or bioinformatics. The role requires strong teamwork and responsibility, and is initially offered on a one-year contract basis with potential renewal depending on performance. The position is distinctive for its emphasis on bridging research discoveries with clinical applications in cardiovascular medicine.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
description: This full-time postdoctoral position within a translation research centre focuses on Chinese translation history, requiring a PhD in this field or a related discipline and a demonstrated track record of research publication. Primary responsibilities include producing articles for international publications, supporting the centre director's research initiatives, helping to organize academic events, and building collaborative networks within the discipline. Candidates must demonstrate capacity for multidisciplinary work and provide a resume, cover letter, academic credentials, and at least one reference letter. The initial one-year contract position is renewable based on performance and mutual agreement, with the start date flexible.

title: Research Fellow
description: This Research Fellow position in the Department of Electronic Engineering seeks a highly experienced researcher specializing in antenna engineering, microwave engineering, radar systems, or synthetic aperture radars, with a Ph.D. and at least eight years of postqualification experience in these fields. The successful candidate will conduct independent, top-tier research under professorial supervision, contribute to postgraduate student mentoring, and demonstrate excellence through publications and a strong academic record. Key requirements include fluency in English, organizational skills, self-motivation, and proven expertise preferably in antenna engineering or synthetic aperture radar systems. The role is offered initially on a one-year contract renewable by mutual agreement.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
department: Joint CUHK-NTEC Clinical Research Management Office
description: This Research Assistant position supports clinical research operations at a hospital research centre, requiring a bachelor's degree in biological science or health-related fields and fluent English and Chinese communication skills. Key responsibilities include organizing clinical research projects, coordinating studies, collecting and managing clinical data, performing site monitoring and start-up activities, and providing general administrative support. The role particularly values strong interpersonal skills, responsibility, self-motivation, and MS Office proficiency, with clinical research experience preferred but not mandatory. The appointment is a one-year renewable contract position.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s)
description: Postdoctoral fellows are sought to join a pharmacy research team, developing natural language processing algorithms to extract content from Chinese and English articles. Candidates must hold a PhD in Pharmacy, Computer Science, or related fields from a reputable institution, with proficiency in at least one programming language and fluent English and Chinese communication skills. Key responsibilities include programming algorithm implementation, leading research projects, securing external funding, training team members, and preparing research documentation. The position offers a one-year renewable contract contingent on performance.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s)
department: Department of Ophthalmology and Visual Sciences
description: The Department of Ophthalmology and Visual Sciences seeks postdoctoral fellows to support clinical and research programs across diverse ophthalmic specialties, including retinal diseases, glaucoma, ocular cancers, and genetic eye conditions. Candidates must hold a PhD in Biological Sciences (preferably ophthalmology or cancer research), demonstrate proficiency in molecular and cell biology techniques, and show strong independent and collaborative research abilities. Key responsibilities include coordinating research projects, analyzing data, preparing manuscripts and grant applications, and training junior staff. The position is offered on a one to two-year renewable contract, with preference given to applicants experienced in next-generation sequencing.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
department: Centre for University and School Partnership
description: This Research Assistant position supports a university center focused on school partnerships, requiring a Bachelor's degree in Social Sciences or education with strong bilingual academic writing skills (English and Chinese) and knowledge of Hong Kong's educational context. The role involves assisting with research and evaluation of centre projects, compiling reports and deliverables, and providing project support, with opportunities for those with prior experience in development programme research or evaluation. Applicants must demonstrate self-motivation, organizational ability, and strong interpersonal skills, though less experienced candidates may be considered for a junior-level appointment. The position is initially offered on a one-year renewable contract basis.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
description: Applicants should hold a Bachelor of Science degree in Biology, Environmental Science, Marine Science, Ecology, or related fields (one to two years of work experience preferred), with proficiency in Microsoft Office, fluent bilingual communication in Chinese and English, strong analytical ability, and demonstrated interest in marine and coral conservation education, with scuba diving certification and prior educational experience highly valued. The position involves organizing and delivering educational programs for secondary schools, conducting conservation talks, planning workshops, coordinating with external stakeholders, managing program budgets and finances, producing educational materials, and maintaining social media presence related to marine and coral conservation efforts. The role requires independent work capabilities and occasional weekend or outdoor work availability. The initial appointment is a one-year renewable contract beginning as soon as possible, with Associate degree holders and fresh graduates also encouraged to apply.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s)
description: Multiple postdoctoral positions are available in the Department of Electronic Engineering focused on smart materials and devices, including fabrication, thermal management, sensing and camouflage technologies, flexible electronics, and soft grippers. Candidates must hold a PhD in STEM from a top 100 institution with background in Materials Science, Energy, Electronic Engineering, or related fields, demonstrated by strong academic records and publications; hands-on fabrication experience is preferred. Applicants should possess excellent problem-solving and analytical skills, strong writing ability, teamwork orientation, and self-motivation. Positions are offered initially on a one-year contract basis, renewable by mutual agreement, with applications requiring submission of CV, transcripts, representative publications, and demo projects.

title: Research Assistant
department: Department of Sports Science and Physical Education
description: This full-time research assistant position requires a Bachelor's degree or higher in Physical Education, Sports Science, Exercise Science, Rehabilitation, Public Health, or related fields, along with fluency in written and spoken English and Chinese. The role involves recruiting school participants, collecting and analyzing research data, organizing and delivering physical activity interventions and workshops, and supporting other departmental tasks. Strong communication skills and the ability to work both independently and collaboratively are essential, with prior experience working with children and youth with special educational needs being advantageous. The position is offered initially on a one-year contract, renewable based on performance and mutual agreement.

title: Full-time/Part-time Research Associate
department: Hong Kong Institute of Asia-Pacific Studies
description: This role involves supervising a team of telephone interviewers at a survey research laboratory with 60 workstations, overseeing data collection operations during evening shifts (2:30–10:30 pm for full-time or 6:30–10:30 pm for part-time positions). Candidates must hold a Master's degree, possess at least ten years of relevant post-qualification experience, demonstrate fluency in Cantonese and spoken English, and exhibit strong problem-solving and organisational abilities, with survey management experience preferred. The position is initially offered on a one-year contract basis, renewable based on performance and mutual agreement, and supports the laboratory's mission of providing professional survey services to researchers and conducting public opinion polls.

title: Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant role involves conducting laboratory research on cardiomyocyte differentiation, heart development, and heart regeneration using cultured mammalian cells, zebrafish, and mouse models, along with adeno-associated virus techniques, under close supervision of the Principal Investigator. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree in Biomedical Sciences or a related field and possess foundational experience in molecular and cellular biology, biochemistry, and standard laboratory techniques, alongside strong communication skills. Desirable qualifications include recent hands-on laboratory work, proficiency in molecular biology methods, CRISPR/Cas9 expertise, and zebrafish handling experience. The position is initially a one-year fixed-term contract, renewable based on performance and mutual agreement.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
description: This postdoctoral position involves conducting research on cardiomyocyte differentiation, heart development, and regeneration using cultured mammalian cells, zebrafish, and mouse models, with potential involvement in adeno-associated virus work. Candidates must hold a Ph.D. in Biomedical Sciences or related disciplines and demonstrate strong hands-on experience in molecular and cellular biology; experience with CRISPR/Cas9, mouse surgery, or zebrafish handling is advantageous. The fellow will design and execute research projects independently, present findings at conferences, prepare manuscripts for publication, and receive mentoring to develop grant-writing skills for future independent research. The appointment is initially for one year on a contract basis, with potential renewal based on performance.

title: Technical Officer (Digital Construction Recording Project) (S&T)
description: This position supports a Digital Construction Recording project within the School of Science and Technology, focusing on capturing and organizing digital documentation of campus construction activities for educational purposes. Key responsibilities include coordinating with contractors and site teams to execute digital recording schedules, capturing construction activities through photography, video, and 360-degree imaging, and cataloguing the resulting data for accessibility and teaching use. The role requires a degree or higher diploma in construction-related fields such as Civil Engineering, Building Studies, Construction Management, or Surveying. The position is distinctive in combining technical documentation with academic support, requiring both construction industry knowledge and ability to transform site records into educational materials for classroom use.

title: Project Manager (Digital Construction Recording Project) (S&T)
description: This position involves managing a digital documentation initiative for a new campus construction project, with primary responsibility for identifying critical construction phases and executing a comprehensive digital recording strategy that transforms the build into an educational resource for construction-related academic programmes. The role requires close collaboration with construction teams and academic staff to develop recording schedules, capture construction metrics across multiple disciplines, and ensure documentation reflects diverse professional perspectives. Key responsibilities include supervising a Technical Officer, maintaining digital equipment and data infrastructure, and coordinating with consultants, engineers, and contractors to balance thorough documentation with minimal disruption to ongoing works. The position is distinctive in its dual focus on supporting both construction management and academic teaching through innovative use of digital documentation technology.

title: Assistant Accounting Officer (General Ledger) (FO)
description: The position involves preparing journal entries and supporting financial schedules, assisting in developing standard operating procedures for general ledger workflows, and performing other assigned duties within the Finance Office. Candidates must have at least five HKDSE Level 2 subjects (including English, Chinese, and Mathematics) and preferably three years of accounting experience, with Oracle Finance system knowledge being advantageous. The role offers competitive remuneration including base salary, end-of-contract gratuity, discretionary incentive payments, and comprehensive benefits such as annual leave, professional development sponsorships, medical and dental coverage, and life insurance.

title: Development and Alumni Affairs Officer (DAAO)
description: The Development and Alumni Affairs Officer supports a modern applied sciences university's fundraising and alumni engagement efforts through planning and coordinating donor cultivation activities, managing alumni communications platforms and databases, and providing administrative support to the office and the university's alumni association. The ideal candidate holds a bachelor's degree with at least two years of professional experience, demonstrates strong bilingual proficiency in English, Cantonese, and Putonghua, and possesses excellent organizational and interpersonal skills alongside competent IT capabilities and digital marketing knowledge. The role requires the ability to work independently with meticulous attention to detail, manage multiple projects simultaneously, and occasional travel outside Hong Kong. This position is distinctive for its focus on connecting the university's applied sciences mission to alumni and donor engagement while supporting the institution's expansion strategy into the Greater Bay Area.

title: Senior Manager (Human Resources) (HRO)
description: This role is a Senior Manager position in the Human Resources Office, reporting to the Director of Human Resources, responsible for assisting in formulating and implementing HR strategies and policies aligned with institutional goals. Key responsibilities include leading an HR team to deliver services to Schools and Offices, providing administrative support to committees, overseeing employee complaints and disciplinary matters, and enhancing HR processes through proactive identification of improvements. The ideal candidate holds at least a bachelor's degree (preferably higher) in Human Resources Management, Business Administration, or a related field, and demonstrates excellent leadership, problem-solving, and analytical capabilities. This is a distinctive opportunity to shape HR functions within a growing applied sciences university expanding into the Greater Bay Area region.

title: Student Affairs Manager (SAO)
description: This role manages the Student Affairs Office's First Year Experience Programme, serving as its primary architect to foster student engagement and smooth transitions into university life. The position requires driving continuous improvement across all SAO programmes through assessment frameworks, data-driven insights, and operational excellence while developing performance indicators to measure effectiveness. The manager will coordinate global learning opportunities by collaborating with academic departments and external partners to deliver international experiences. Additional responsibilities include supporting evidence-based practices among staff and performing duties assigned by the Director of Student Affairs.

title: Assistant Librarian I / II (Library Systems and Technology) (LIB)
description: This position oversees the daily operations and strategic development of library systems and technology, including administration of integrated library management systems, discovery portals, link resolvers, and related digital infrastructure. The Assistant Librarian is responsible for planning technology initiatives, formulating systems policies and procedures, and evaluating emerging technologies to enhance library services while also contributing to collection development and academic liaison work. Candidates must hold a relevant qualification and possess 2-6 years of post-qualification experience in library systems within an academic setting, with strong technical knowledge of integrated library systems and digital platforms being essential. The role requires both technical expertise and service-oriented thinking to support the institution's teaching, research, and community engagement mission.

title: Public Affairs Officer (Multimedia Design) (PAO)
description: This position involves designing and producing multimedia content including short videos, social media graphics, motion graphics, and animated advertisements to support the institution's digital marketing and social media presence across platforms like Facebook, Instagram, and Xiaohongshu. The role requires collaborating with internal teams to develop innovative visual content aligned with brand strategy, monitoring emerging trends in digital design, and maintaining basic website functionality using WordPress. Key requirements include strong skills in video production, graphic design, motion graphics, and social media content creation, with the ability to work creatively within established brand guidelines. The distinctive aspect of this role is its focus on applied multimedia design that directly supports institutional branding and community engagement in a rapidly growing institution expanding into the Greater Bay Area.

title: Research Associate (Digital Health) (N&HS)
department: School of Nursing and Health Sciences
description: The Research Associate position supports digital health projects in the School of Nursing and Health Sciences, focusing on wearable devices, mobile applications, and health sensors for personalized patient care. Key responsibilities include managing project implementation, designing AI-based prediction tools and applications, building hardware and software platforms, coordinating team members, and conducting quantitative and qualitative data analysis including database construction. The ideal candidate holds a PhD in Electronic Engineering, Computer Engineering/Science, Medical Engineering, or a related discipline, with proficiency in backend frameworks such as PHP, Node.js, or Python, alongside frontend development skills. This role is distinctive for its integration of advanced digital technologies, artificial intelligence, and healthcare applications within an applied sciences research context focused on solving real-world health challenges.

title: Assistant Finance Manager (Procurement) (FO)
description: The Assistant Finance Manager (Procurement) position supports the Finance Manager in leading a procurement team while managing day-to-day operations and corporate insurance programs. Key responsibilities include independently handling procurement projects, overseeing inventory and fixed assets, developing sourcing strategies, and optimizing procurement systems to support strategic business objectives. Candidates should hold a degree in purchasing, supply management, or business administration with at least three years of relevant post-qualification experience in procurement, tendering, and vendor management, preferably from larger organizations. The role requires strong planning and problem-solving abilities, proficiency in English and Chinese, and high computer literacy, particularly with Microsoft Office and procurement systems.

title: Research Assistant II (R6586) (N&HS)
department: School of Nursing and Health Sciences
description: This Research Assistant II position supports an Exergaming Research Centre project focused on healthy aging in the School of Nursing and Health Sciences. Key responsibilities include assisting with data collection and analysis, database management, preparing study materials, and providing instruction and support to older adult participants while monitoring their engagement. Candidates should hold a Bachelor's degree or higher in Psychology, Education Psychology, or a health-related field, with preferred experience in fNIRS data collection and analysis, plus strong communication and problem-solving skills; prior research experience with older adults is advantageous. The role offers competitive remuneration including salary, gratuity, generous leave, professional development support, and comprehensive benefits.

title: Assistant Finance Manager (Payment) (FO)
description: This position involves supervising a payment operations team and managing accounts payable functions for the Finance Office, including payment scheduling, reconciliations, month-end closing processes, and audit support. The successful candidate must hold a recognized degree in Accountancy or related field with at least 3 years of post-qualification experience in sizable organizations, with professional accounting qualifications (such as CPA) highly preferred. Key competencies include strong analytical and organizational abilities, meticulous attention to detail, the capacity to manage multiple deadlines under pressure, and excellent communication skills in English and Chinese. The role is distinctive for its emphasis on process improvement and system automation initiatives to enhance departmental efficiency.

title: Research Assistant I/II (Project Ref. No.: CS/106/2412/RA) (S&T)
description: The position supports a research project developing a driving performance analysis system for driving tests, with primary responsibilities including frontend user interface implementation using modern frameworks and backend server logic/API development in collaboration with the engineering team. Candidates must hold a Master's degree or above in a computing-related discipline (Electronic Engineering, Computer Science, AI, Software Engineering, etc.) for Research Assistant I, or a Bachelor's degree for Research Assistant II in the same fields. Full-stack developer experience and a valid driving licence are advantageous but not required. The role offers competitive remuneration including base salary and end-of-contract gratuity.

title: Assistant Finance Manager (Financial Accounting) (FO)
description: This position seeks a qualified accountant with at least 4 years of post-qualification experience to lead the Financial Accounting team and oversee day-to-day general accounting operations for the university. Key responsibilities include preparing consolidated financial statements and management accounts, handling externally-funded project reporting, and implementing process improvements to enhance operational efficiency. The ideal candidate is self-motivated, organized, and able to thrive in a dynamic, pressured environment while demonstrating strong communication skills and a service-oriented mindset. The university offers competitive remuneration including salary, gratuity, discretionary incentives, generous leave, professional development opportunities, and comprehensive benefits including medical, dental, and life insurance coverage.

title: Assistant Industry Partnerships Manager (SAO)
description: This role involves managing and developing industry partnership initiatives for the Student Affairs Office, including coordinating with internal departments, engaging existing partners across corporate, government, and non-profit sectors, and identifying new collaboration opportunities in areas such as career placements, entrepreneurship, and curriculum development. The position requires conducting industry research to inform partnership strategy and creating marketing materials to promote institutional capabilities. Candidates must hold a bachelor's degree and demonstrate at least 6 years of relevant experience in building partnership initiatives in Hong Kong and mainland China, with proven expertise in post-secondary industry partnerships and student development. The role is distinctive for its focus on connecting academic institutions with diverse external stakeholders to enhance student outcomes and align education with market needs in the context of an applied sciences university.

title: Assistant Academic Support Officer (S&T)
description: This Assistant Academic Support Officer position in the School of Science and Technology involves providing operational support to daily school functions, including academic programmes, quality assurance, research, and projects, while handling human resources matters and liaising with internal and external stakeholders. Candidates must hold at least 5 HKDSE Level 2 subjects (including English, Chinese, and Mathematics) or equivalent, with preferably some administrative or support services experience, and must demonstrate strong bilingual communication skills in English and Chinese (including Putonghua). The role requires self-motivation, quick learning ability, independent work capability, and a strong sense of responsibility. The package includes competitive salary, end-of-contract gratuity, annual leave, professional development sponsorship, and comprehensive health and insurance benefits.

title: Assistant Human Resources Officer (HRO)
description: The Assistant Human Resources Officer position in the Human Resources Office involves providing support across specialised HR functions including compensation and benefits, performance management, talent development, staffing, and strategic workforce planning, alongside maintaining accurate HR records. Candidates must possess at least five Level 2 HKDSE subjects (including English, Chinese, and Mathematics) or equivalent, with a recognised degree in Human Resources Management or related field preferred. Strong bilingual communication skills in English and Chinese (with Putonghua preferred) are essential. The role offers competitive compensation including basic salary, end-of-contract gratuity, discretionary incentive payments, generous annual leave, professional development sponsorships, and comprehensive medical, dental, and life insurance benefits.

title: Senior Lecturer / Lecturer (Digital Business) (LiPACE)
department: Li Ka Shing School of Professional and Continuing Education
description: The role is a Senior Lecturer or Lecturer position in Digital Business within a professional and continuing education school focused on vocational training and lifelong learning. The post holder will be responsible for developing and delivering digital business curriculum, supporting students across diverse age groups and backgrounds, and contributing to research in applied digital business areas aligned with institutional priorities. Key requirements include expertise in digital business, strong teaching capabilities suitable for non-traditional learners, and the ability to bridge academic knowledge with practical industry applications. This position is distinctive for its focus on vocational and professional education rather than traditional academic pathways, serving mature learners and working professionals seeking career advancement and skill development.

title: Lecturer (Health Care) (LiPACE)
department: Li Ka Shing School of Professional and Continuing Education
description: This position offers a Lecturer role in healthcare within the School of Professional and Continuing Education, responsible for teaching foundational medical science courses including basic pharmacology and common medicines to vocational and professional education students. The role requires expertise in healthcare education combined with a commitment to serving diverse, non-traditional learners across various age groups and backgrounds. The position is distinctive in its focus on applied sciences and professional training within an institution pioneering vocational education in Hong Kong, with potential connections to Greater Bay Area initiatives. The ideal candidate will support both full-time post-secondary and part-time continuing professional education programmes while contributing to the institution's mission of providing accessible, quality education across the Hong Kong community.

title: Senior Lecturer / Lecturer (Business Administration and Management) (LiPACE)
department: Li Ka Shing School of Professional and Continuing Education
description: A professional education institution seeks a Senior Lecturer or Lecturer in Business Administration and Management to join its School of Professional and Continuing Education. The role is distinctive for supporting vocational and professional education delivery across full-time post-secondary and part-time professional programs, alongside corporate training initiatives. The position will contribute to the school's mission of providing accessible, quality education to diverse learners of varying ages, backgrounds, and career stages. The ideal candidate will align with the institution's strategic focus on applied skills, innovative teaching, and preparing talent to meet Hong Kong's evolving industry needs.

title: Lecturer (Health and Molecular Testing) (LiPACE)
department: Li Ka Shing School of Professional and Continuing Education
description: This position is for a Lecturer in Health and Molecular Testing within the professional and continuing education division of an applied sciences university. The role involves teaching and curriculum development in health and molecular testing across both full-time and part-time professional education programmes. Key requirements would include expertise in health and molecular testing, teaching experience in vocational or professional education, and the ability to contribute to programme development aligned with industry standards. The distinctive aspect is the focus on applied, practice-oriented education serving diverse adult learners through flexible study modes within an institutional context committed to vocational excellence and community engagement.

title: Associate Professor / Assistant Professor (Nursing) (N&HS)
department: School of Nursing and Health Sciences
description: This position seeks an Associate or Assistant Professor in nursing to lead course planning, development, and delivery while contributing to research and institutional service within a faculty-driven, student-centred university. Candidates must demonstrate strong academic leadership, teaching excellence, innovation in educational delivery and curriculum design, a solid publication record in peer-reviewed outlets, success in securing external research funding, and meaningful contributions to the university, profession and community. The role emphasizes applied professional education aligned with industry needs and strategic research areas including gerontechnology and personalised care. Appointment level and a competitive benefits package including salary, allowances, and professional development support will be determined based on qualifications and experience.

title: Associate Professor / Assistant Professor (Mental Health) (N&HS)
department: School of Nursing and Health Sciences
description: The position seeks an Associate or Assistant Professor in Mental Health for the School of Nursing and Health Sciences to undertake course planning, teaching delivery, research leadership, and institutional service. Candidates must hold a PhD in Mental Health or a related discipline, demonstrate academic leadership and strong teaching capabilities, possess a solid publication record in peer-reviewed venues, and show evidence of securing external research funding. The university particularly values innovation in curriculum design, postgraduate student supervision, and contributions to the academic profession and broader community. Appointment level will depend on the candidate's experience and achievements in these areas.

title: Senior Lecturer / Lecturer (Mental Health) (N&HS)
department: School of Nursing and Health Sciences
description: This position is for a Senior Lecturer or Lecturer in Mental Health Nursing within the School of Nursing and Health Sciences, responsible for course coordination, clinical assessments, mentoring, classroom teaching, and scholarly research activities. The ideal candidate must hold professional registration/licensure as a mental health nurse with at least three years of professional experience, and a track record in relevant research. Appointment level and remuneration depend on the candidate's academic qualifications, leadership experience, publications, and research record, with benefits including competitive salary, gratuity, generous leave, professional development support, and medical/dental coverage. Overseas candidates under age 50 may be eligible for housing support through a charitable trust programme.

title: Senior Lecturer / Lecturer (Clinical) (N&HS)
department: School of Nursing and Health Sciences
description: This position invites a nursing professional to serve as a Senior Lecturer or Lecturer (Clinical) in the School of Nursing and Health Sciences, with primary responsibilities encompassing course coordination, clinical assessments, mentoring, and classroom teaching in nursing and related disciplines. Candidates must hold professional registration with relevant nursing boards and possess at least five years of professional experience, preferably in Adult and Child Nursing, Mental Health Nursing, or other clinical nursing specialties. The role requires engagement in scholarly activities, academic research, and participation in institutional administration and outreach. The appointment level and remuneration package are contingent on the candidate's academic credentials, teaching experience, research track record, and demonstrated academic leadership.

title: Part-time Senior Lecturer/Lecturer (Physiotherapy) (N&HS)
department: School of Nursing and Health Sciences
description: This part-time position seeks a Senior Lecturer or Lecturer in Physiotherapy to assist with course planning, development, coordination, and delivery across the institution's physiotherapy programmes at multiple academic levels. Candidates must hold a Master's degree or higher in Physiotherapy or a related field, with at least five years of post-qualification professional experience, proficiency in Microsoft Office applications, and preferably prior higher education teaching experience. The role involves fewer than 68 hours of work per four-week period on a temporary contract through June 2026, with statutory benefits provided.

title: Course Developer (Freelance) (LANG 3331AED and LANG 3332AED) (A&SS)
department: School of Arts and Social Sciences
description: This freelance position involves developing two sociolinguistics courses (LANG 3331AED and LANG 3332AED) for a School of Arts and Social Sciences, with primary responsibilities including writing course units aligned with learning outcomes, creating self-tests and activities, and designing assessments that allow students to apply learned skills and concepts. The ideal candidate holds a higher degree in Linguistics with specialization in Sociolinguistics or a closely related field, and should demonstrate excellent organizational skills, meticulous attention to detail, and ability to work under pressure. Teaching, research, and course design experience are advantageous. The role is offered on a freelance basis.

title: Part-time Lecturer of EDU 3355ECF (2026 Summer Term) (E&L)
description: This part-time lecturer position involves teaching EDU 3355ECF during the 2026 summer term within the School of Education and Languages, serving undergraduate degree programmes in Chinese language teaching, applied Chinese language, and related fields. Candidates must have a minimum of five years of experience in student discipline, guidance, and pastoral care roles within local secondary or primary schools. Teaching experience or teacher training qualifications are preferred. The role is suited for experienced education professionals seeking part-time work aligned with the university's commitment to applied professional education and community-focused teaching.

title: Part-time Research Assistants I /II (Project No. R7043) (A&SS)
department: School of Arts and Social Sciences
description: This part-time position involves assisting researchers on a longitudinal study examining how poor housing conditions affect child development in Hong Kong's subdivided units, using mixed-method analysis. Key responsibilities include coordinating with academic staff and NGO partners, conducting quantitative and qualitative research, and supporting project operations, with occasional weekend and evening work required. Candidates must have research experience, proficiency in both English and Cantonese, strong computer skills including data analysis software (NVivo), and the ability to work independently under deadline pressure. The role is contracted for 16.5 hours per week from March to June 2026.

title: Part-time Lecturer (PG) (2026 Summer) (A&SS Creative Arts)
department: School of Arts and Social Sciences
description: This part-time position involves teaching and curriculum development for postgraduate creative arts programmes during summer 2026 (May-August), including lecturing, tutoring, grading assessments, and designing complementary course topics. Applicants must hold a higher degree in Creative Writing, Chinese Language and Literature, or related field, with tertiary-level teaching experience and professional expertise; PhD holders are preferred. Candidates must be fluent in both Cantonese and Putonghua and should be willing to work daytime and/or evening shifts on campus, with the contract typically requiring under 68 hours per four-week period. The role emphasizes the institution's commitment to applied sciences education and innovative teaching within a student-centred university framework.

title: Part-time Research Assistant II (R6887) (N&HS)
department: School of Nursing and Health Sciences
description: This part-time position supports laboratory-based research in the School of Nursing and Health Sciences, involving conducting experiments with DNA/RNA sequencing techniques, preparing research reports under supervision, and assisting with related research activities as directed by the Principal Investigator. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree or higher in Biological, Biomedical, or Biotechnology-related sciences with demonstrated practical experience in molecular biology techniques such as DNA/RNA extraction, cell culture, PCR, gel electrophoresis, and Western blotting. The ideal candidate is detail-oriented, self-motivated, reliable with deadlines, and capable of independent work. The position is offered as a temporary part-time contract with statutory benefits.

title: Temporary Research Assistant II (N&HS)
department: School of Nursing and Health Sciences
description: This temporary full-time position (3-6 months) in the School of Nursing and Health Sciences supports health economics research projects, requiring the appointee to coordinate project logistics, design surveys and promotional materials, recruit participants, conduct interviews and focus groups, and manage data entry for qualitative and quantitative datasets. The ideal candidate holds a bachelor's degree or higher in social sciences or related fields, with strong proficiency in Traditional Chinese (written) and Cantonese (spoken), typing skills in both Chinese and English, and demonstrated expertise in Canvas and Qualtrics platforms, with additional knowledge of R and STATA preferred. Essential personal qualities include self-motivation, independence, and flexibility to work beyond standard weekday hours when required. This role is distinctive in its emphasis on health economics research methodology combined with community engagement and multilingual communication skills.

title: Temporary Laboratory Officer (N&HS)
department: School of Nursing and Health Sciences
description: This temporary full-time position supports the School of Nursing and Health Sciences by preparing laboratory materials and equipment, demonstrating experiments, and providing instrument training to students. The successful candidate should hold a Bachelor's degree in a related biomedical discipline (such as Medical Laboratory Science or Microbiology) with a minimum of two years' experience supporting teaching in laboratory settings; MLT registration and specialized experience in areas like Clinical Chemistry or Medical Microbiology are preferred. Key responsibilities include ensuring laboratory safety and security, while the ideal candidate will be detail-oriented, independent, and capable of meeting deadlines. This role offers statutory benefits and is filled through the university's eRecruitment system.

title: Course Developer (Master of Science in Diagnostic Ultrasonography) (Freelance) (N&HS)
department: School of Nursing and Health Sciences
description: This freelance position involves developing course materials and examination questions for a new Master of Science in Diagnostic Ultrasonography programme offered in English. The role requires a higher degree in Health Sciences, radiology, or medical imaging, with preferred experience in distance learning or part-time course instruction and prior involvement in course material development for higher education institutions. The programme focuses on comprehensive ultrasonography training, emphasizing abdominal and vascular imaging while introducing broader imaging modalities, and the course developer will support the School of Nursing and Health Sciences in creating educational content that builds students' technical proficiency and clinical knowledge.

title: Temporary Clerk (REG)
description: This is a temporary full-time clerical position in the Registry department responsible for delivering frontline student services across multiple channels including counter, email, and telephone support, as well as assisting with admissions and enrolment functions. Candidates must meet Hong Kong secondary education qualifications (HKDSE Level 2 or HKCEE Grade E or above in at least 5 subjects including English and Chinese), demonstrate proficiency in English and Chinese with preferred Putonghua capability, and possess competent digital skills in Microsoft Office applications. The role offers staff development opportunities within a faculty-driven, student-centred university that emphasizes applied sciences and professional education. Employment is on a temporary full-time basis with statutory benefits.

title: Temporary Assistant Officer (HR team) (N&HS)
department: School of Nursing and Health Sciences
description: This is a temporary full-time assistant officer position within the human resources team of the School of Nursing and Health Sciences. Candidates should hold a recognized degree (preferably in human resources management or related field) and demonstrate strong proficiency in English, Chinese, and mathematics at HKDSE Level 2 or equivalent, along with excellent computer, organizational, and interpersonal skills in both English and Chinese languages. The role supports HR functions within a modern university focused on professional education and strategic research across areas including gerontechnology, personalized care, and smart city initiatives. Employment will be offered on a temporary contract with statutory benefits provided.

title: Part-time Research Associate / Assistant I/II / Technical Assistant (Project No: R2091) (B&A)
department: Lee Shau Kee School of Business and Administration
description: This is a part-time research position within the School of Business and Administration supporting a project investigating gender inequality among esports practitioners. The role involves participant recruitment, data collection, interview transcription, qualitative data coding using NVivo, and report preparation under the Principal Investigator's direction. Candidates require a Bachelor's degree in Sport Management, Sport Marketing, Sport Psychology, or related social science discipline, with demonstrated experience in qualitative research methods, proficiency in English and Chinese (with Mandarin transcription as an advantage), and strong attention to detail and self-motivation.

title: Temporary Research Assistant II (R6943) (S&T)
description: This temporary research assistant position supports a project investigating per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS) in urban stormwater through chemical profiling and bioactivity analysis. The role involves providing laboratory and technical support, conducting environmental sampling and sample preparation, assisting with report writing, and performing other research activities under the Principal Investigator's direction. Applicants must hold a bachelor's degree in Chemistry, Analytical Chemistry, Environmental Science, or related field, with preferred experience in laboratory analytical work and instrumental analysis techniques such as LC-MS/MS and GC-MS. Successful candidates should be detail-oriented, self-motivated, responsible, and capable of meeting deadlines independently, and will be appointed on a temporary full-time contract with statutory benefits.

title: Temporary Facilities Officer (Interior Design) (11-month contract) (FMO)
description: This is an 11-month temporary full-time position in the Facilities Management Office responsible for interior design-related facilities work. Candidates must have at least five HKDSE subjects at Level 2 or above (including English, Chinese, and Mathematics) and preferably a recognized degree, along with strong bilingual communication skills in English and Chinese. The role offers statutory benefits and requires submission through the university's eRecruitment System, with applicants notified of outcomes within eight weeks of the closing date.

title: Part-Time Research Assistant I (Psychology of Sports, Exercise, and Health) (A&SS)
department: School of Arts and Social Sciences
description: This is a part-time research assistant position supporting faculty-led research in sports psychology, physical activity, and health education. The role involves coordinating mental health workshops at schools and community settings, preparing experimental materials, liaising with schools and NGOs, and assisting with data collection on flexible schedules including evenings and weekends. Candidates must hold a Master's degree in psychology, social sciences, sports sciences, or health-related fields, with demonstrated experience in quantitative and qualitative research methods, proficiency in Microsoft Office and data analysis software, and strong bilingual communication skills in English and Cantonese.

title: Temporary Research Assistant I (R6671) (S&T)
description: This temporary full-time position assists a research team investigating sentiment analysis to distinguish between ChatGPT-generated and human-generated text within the School of Science and Technology. The role primarily involves preparing research papers and reports, supporting the Principal Investigator, and performing related research duties. Candidates must hold a Master's degree or higher in Engineering, Computer Science, Education Technology, or related fields, with demonstrated research experience in machine learning, artificial intelligence, data labeling, natural language processing, and computer vision. Proficiency in English communication and writing is preferred.

title: Part-time Lecturer (AI and healthcare technologies) [2026 Summer Term] (N&HS)
department: School of Nursing and Health Sciences
description: This is a part-time teaching position in artificial intelligence and healthcare technologies for the summer term, based in the School of Nursing and Health Sciences. The lecturer will deliver courses covering foundational knowledge and practical skills in AI and smart healthcare technologies, including lectures, tutorials, course development, and student assessment, with classes typically held in the evenings and a maximum workload of 68 hours per four-week cycle. The ideal candidate holds a doctoral degree in computer science, health informatics, artificial intelligence, data science, or a related field, with demonstrated tertiary-level teaching experience and proficiency in big data analytics. This role supports the institution's strategic focus on gerontechnology and personalised care while contributing to professional education in an applied sciences context.

title: Part-time Lecturer for EDU 2201ECF (2026 Summer Term)
description: This is a part-time lecturer position for a summer term course (EDU 2201ECF) within the School of Education and Languages, teaching in either the Bachelor of Education (Putonghua and Chinese Education) or Bachelor of Arts (Chinese Linguistics) degree programmes. The role requires delivering course content to full-time students during the 2026 summer term on a part-time basis. Candidates should have relevant expertise in education, Chinese language teaching, or linguistics depending on the specific course assignment. The position is temporary and limited to the summer term duration, with applications considered unsuccessful if candidates are not contacted within eight weeks of the closing date.

title: Temporary / Part-time Research Assistant / Technical Assistant (Project No: R6839) (E&L)
description: This temporary part-time position in the School of Education and Languages involves assisting a Principal Investigator on a research project examining ethical AI use in education, with responsibilities including designing research instruments, preparing reports, and performing assigned duties.
